report_text: The patient underwent a total laryngectomy with bilateral selective neck dissection due to a T3 squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. The tumor was primarily located at the glottic level, causing significant airway obstruction. Intraoperative findings revealed tumor extension to the supraglottic region without involvement of the thyroid cartilage. Bilateral cervical lymph nodes were enlarged, and metastatic involvement was confirmed in the ipsilateral level II and III nodes. The procedure was completed with tracheoesophageal puncture for voice rehabilitation.

A wide local excision of the floor of the mouth and partial glossectomy was performed for a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the oral cavity. The tumor had infiltrated the anterior two-thirds of the tongue and extended to the sublingual gland. Margins were confirmed clear by frozen section analysis. No clinical or radiological evidence of lymph node metastasis was observed. Reconstruction was achieved using a radial forearm free flap.

A patient with a diagnosis of T1 carcinoma of the nasopharynx underwent endoscopic resection of the tumor. The tumor was confined to the nasopharyngeal mucosa with no evidence of skull base invasion. No lymphadenopathy was detected on preoperative imaging, and intraoperative inspection confirmed the absence of regional spread. The surgical site was closed primarily without complications.

The patient presented with a T4a carcinoma of the hypopharynx involving the pyriform sinus, necessitating a total pharyngolaryngectomy with bilateral neck dissection. The tumor had invaded the thyroid gland, necessitating a total thyroidectomy. Multiple ipsilateral cervical lymph nodes exhibited metastatic disease, specifically in levels II-IV. The surgical field was reconstructed using a pectoralis major myocutaneous flap.

A partial maxillectomy was conducted for a T3 malignant neoplasm of the maxillary sinus. The tumor had eroded the anterior wall of the sinus and extended into the oral cavity, involving the hard palate. There were no signs of intracranial extension or lymph node metastasis on imaging. A dental obturator was fashioned postoperatively to facilitate oral function and speech.

The patient, a 67-year-old male with a history of tobacco use, presented with a progressively worsening hoarseness and difficulty swallowing. On examination, a mass was identified in the left supraglottic region. Direct laryngoscopy revealed a 3 cm exophytic tumor involving the epiglottis and extending to the aryepiglottic fold. A supraglottic laryngectomy with partial pharyngectomy was performed. Intraoperative frozen section analysis confirmed negative margins. Selective neck dissection was conducted, with two out of 15 lymph nodes positive for metastatic squamous cell carcinoma.

A 52-year-old female presented with a non-healing ulcer on the right lateral border of the tongue. Biopsy revealed a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma. The patient underwent a partial glossectomy with reconstruction using a radial forearm free flap. Margins were clear of the tumor. A neck dissection was performed, and no lymph nodes showed evidence of metastasis. The surgical site was closed in layers, and the patient was transferred to the recovery room in stable condition.

The patient is a 60-year-old male with a 3-month history of right-sided throat pain and dysphagia. Imaging and endoscopic evaluation revealed a mass on the right tonsillar fossa, with biopsy confirming squamous cell carcinoma. A transoral robotic surgery (TORS) was performed with resection of the right tonsil and involved peritonsillar tissues. The procedure included a right modified radical neck dissection, and metastatic involvement was noted in four out of 30 lymph nodes. Postoperative recovery was uneventful.

A 45-year-old male presented with a painless swelling in the left neck and a sensation of fullness in the throat. Imaging studies showed a 4 cm mass in the left base of tongue, infiltrating the lingual tonsil. Underwent a transoral laser microsurgery with resection of the tumor and left-sided neck dissection. Pathology confirmed poorly differentiated squamous cell carcinoma with extracapsular spread in three lymph nodes. The surgical margins were negative.

The patient, a 58-year-old female, was referred with a persistent sore in the floor of the mouth. Examination and biopsy revealed a 2 cm squamous cell carcinoma involving the anterior floor of mouth. The treatment involved a wide local excision of the lesion with primary closure. A sentinel lymph node biopsy was performed, which showed no metastatic involvement. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was discharged with instructions for follow-up care.

The patient, a 58-year-old male, underwent a partial glossectomy for a well-defined squamous cell carcinoma located on the lateral border of the tongue. The lesion measured 2.5 cm in greatest dimension and had infiltrated into the surrounding musculature. A selective neck dissection was performed, revealing metastatic involvement in two of the ipsilateral lymph nodes. The surgical margins were clear, and there were no perioperative complications. The patient was extubated successfully and transferred to the recovery unit in stable condition.

A 63-year-old female presented with a history of hoarseness and progressive dysphagia. Imaging and biopsy confirmed the presence of a T3 squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. The patient underwent a total laryngectomy with bilateral neck dissection. Intraoperative findings included tumor infiltration into the thyroid cartilage but no extralaryngeal spread. Pathology confirmed clear surgical margins, and of the 30 lymph nodes excised, three on the right side were positive for malignancy. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, with the patient adapting well to alternative communication methods.

The patient, a 45-year-old male, presented with a rapidly enlarging mass in the left tonsil. Examination and imaging suggested a malignant tumor, and a biopsy confirmed poorly differentiated squamous cell carcinoma. The patient underwent a radical tonsillectomy with ipsilateral neck dissection. The tumor was extensive, measuring 4 cm, with invasion into adjacent muscle tissue. Multiple lymph nodes were positive for metastatic spread. Despite the complexity, the surgical team achieved clear margins. The patient was stable in the postoperative period and scheduled for adjuvant therapy.

A 70-year-old female was diagnosed with a 1.5 cm basal cell carcinoma on the left lower lip. Due to the size and location, a wide local excision was performed, ensuring clear margins. The defect was reconstructed using a local advancement flap under local anesthesia. There was no evidence of lymphatic involvement, and the patient tolerated the procedure well, with minimal postoperative edema and no signs of infection. The patient was discharged home on the same day with instructions for wound care.

The patient, a 52-year-old male, presented with a non-healing ulcer on the floor of the mouth. Biopsy identified a squamous cell carcinoma, prompting a surgical resection of the involved oral cavity floor. The procedure included marginal mandibulectomy due to bone involvement. A neck dissection was conducted, revealing two metastatic nodes in the submandibular region. The surgical margins were clear, but the proximity to bone required careful postoperative monitoring. The patient was extubated without issues and transferred to the surgical ward for recovery.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, presented with a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the glottic larynx. A transoral laser microsurgery was performed, involving a partial laryngectomy to excise the tumor with clear margins. Intraoperatively, the vocal cords were visualized and resected with precision to preserve as much function as possible. The procedure was completed without complications, and the patient was extubated in the operating room. Postoperative care included voice therapy to aid in recovery.

A 55-year-old female with a known history of smoking presented with a mass in the right tonsillar region. Surgical intervention included a right tonsillectomy and selective neck dissection. The tumor, classified as T3, had invaded the surrounding soft tissues but was resected with negative margins. Pathological examination confirmed lymph node involvement in level II, necessitating further oncological assessment for potential adjuvant therapy.

The patient, a 70-year-old male, was diagnosed with a carcinoma of the hypopharynx, specifically involving the postcricoid region. A total laryngopharyngectomy with bilateral neck dissection was executed. Reconstruction was accomplished using a radial forearm free flap to restore the pharyngeal conduit. The procedure was extensive due to the advanced nature of the disease (T4a), with underlying cartilage invasion. The patient was transferred to the ICU for postoperative monitoring.

A 48-year-old female presented with a lesion in the floor of the mouth, identified as squamous cell carcinoma on biopsy. A wide local excision was performed, including partial mandibulectomy due to bone involvement. The surgical procedure also involved the reconstruction of the defect using a fibular free flap. Lymph nodes were sampled from levels I-III, none of which showed metastatic disease. The surgery was followed by placement of a nasogastric tube for nutritional support during recovery.

A 60-year-old male with a firm, palpable mass in the submandibular region underwent surgical resection. The mass was identified as an oropharyngeal carcinoma with metastatic spread to the submandibular lymph nodes. The surgical team performed a radical neck dissection. Intraoperatively, care was taken to preserve the marginal mandibular nerve to maintain lower facial muscle function. The surgery concluded with the placement of drains, and the patient was advised on postoperative radiation therapy.

The patient, a 57-year-old male, underwent a partial glossectomy for a T2 squamous cell carcinoma located on the left lateral border of the tongue. Intraoperatively, the lesion measured approximately 2.5 cm and was excised with clear margins. A selective neck dissection was performed, targeting levels I-III, due to suspected lymphatic involvement. One of the lymph nodes in level II was found to be positive for metastatic carcinoma. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was transferred to the recovery unit in stable condition.

A 63-year-old female presented with a T1 lesion on the buccal mucosa. A wide local excision was performed, removing a 1.2 cm diameter lesion with a 1 cm margin. No lymphadenopathy was detected on pre-operative imaging, and no neck dissection was performed. The surgical site was closed primarily, and the patient was discharged on the same day with instructions for follow-up in the outpatient clinic.

This 70-year-old male was diagnosed with a T3 squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx, involving the vocal cords and extending into the supraglottic region. A total laryngectomy was performed, with bilateral neck dissection due to palpable lymph nodes on both sides. Multiple nodes in levels II and III were positive for metastases. A tracheostomy was also performed, and the patient was admitted to the ICU for postoperative monitoring.

A 45-year-old female underwent a transoral robotic-assisted resection of a T1 oropharyngeal cancer situated on the right tonsillar pillar. The lesion measured 1.5 cm and was excised with a margin of 0.5 cm. Postoperative examination of the cervical lymph nodes revealed no evidence of metastatic spread. The patient was extubated in the operating room and transferred to the recovery unit in stable condition, with plans for adjuvant radiation therapy.

The patient, a 52-year-old male, presented with a T4a carcinoma of the hypopharynx, involving the right pyriform sinus and extending to the postcricoid area. A total pharyngolaryngectomy was performed with radial forearm free flap reconstruction. A comprehensive neck dissection was conducted, and multiple nodes in level IV were identified as metastatic. The patient was stabilized in the ICU postoperatively and will require enteral feeding via a gastrostomy tube.

The patient underwent a total laryngectomy with bilateral neck dissection due to an advanced squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. The tumor was primarily located in the glottic region, with extension to the supraglottic area. Intraoperatively, the tumor was found to invade the thyroid cartilage. A total of 32 lymph nodes were removed from levels II through V on both sides, with metastatic involvement found in 5 nodes on the right side. The procedure was completed without complications, and the patient was transferred to recovery in stable condition.

A 56-year-old female presented with a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the right tonsil. A transoral robotic surgery (TORS) was performed to excise the primary tumor, which measured 2.5 cm in greatest dimension. The tumor was confined to the tonsillar tissue, with no evidence of invasion into the surrounding structures. A selective neck dissection was performed on the ipsilateral side, with 18 lymph nodes removed, all of which were negative for metastatic disease. Margins were clear, and the patient tolerated the procedure well.

The patient underwent a composite resection of the floor of the mouth and anterior tongue due to a clinically T4a squamous cell carcinoma. The tumor involved the anterior two-thirds of the tongue and the floor of the mouth with extension to the mandible. A segmental mandibulectomy was performed, and reconstruction was carried out using a fibula free flap. A left neck dissection was conducted, yielding 28 nodes, 3 of which showed metastatic involvement. Postoperatively, the patient was stable and extubated in the recovery room.

A 63-year-old male with a diagnosis of hypopharyngeal carcinoma underwent partial pharyngectomy with resection of the pyriform sinus. The tumor was localized to the left pyriform sinus and measured 3 cm. A unilateral neck dissection was performed, and 24 lymph nodes were harvested. Pathology confirmed metastatic disease in 2 nodes. No invasion of the esophagus or larynx was noted. The patient was extubated in the operating room and transferred to the surgical ward in stable condition.

The patient had a localized squamous cell carcinoma of the buccal mucosa and underwent wide local excision. The lesion was 1.5 cm and located on the right buccal surface with clear margins achieved. No nodal dissection was performed as there was no clinical or radiographic evidence of lymph node involvement. The patient recovered well and was discharged with instructions for outpatient follow-up.

The patient underwent a total laryngectomy with bilateral neck dissection due to a locally advanced squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. The tumor was primarily located in the glottic region, extending to the supraglottic area. During the procedure, we noted significant invasion into surrounding soft tissues, necessitating extensive resection. Bilateral neck dissection revealed metastatic involvement in multiple cervical lymph nodes. Reconstruction was completed using a radial forearm free flap to ensure a viable airway.

A transoral robotic surgery was performed to excise a T1 oropharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma located in the tonsillar fossa. The tumor was well-circumscribed and confined without evidence of lymph node involvement during imaging or intraoperative assessment. Margins were clear upon frozen section analysis. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was extubated in the operating room.

The patient presented with a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the left floor of the mouth. A composite resection, including the anterior mandible, was necessary due to tumor infiltration. Ipsilateral selective neck dissection was performed, which showed no signs of metastatic spread. The defect was reconstructed with a fibula osteocutaneous free flap. Postoperative recovery was uneventful.

A radical hypopharyngectomy was conducted on a patient with an extensive hypopharyngeal carcinoma, primarily involving the pyriform sinus. The tumor had invaded the postcricoid area, and there was palpable lymphadenopathy in the left neck. A left modified radical neck dissection was performed, which confirmed the presence of metastatic nodes. Reconstruction was achieved using a jejunal free flap.

The patient underwent a wide local excision of a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the buccal mucosa. The surgical team performed a marginal mandibulectomy due to proximity to the bone. Intraoperative frozen sections confirmed clear margins. No lymphadenopathy was detected in the preoperative imaging, and therefore, no neck dissection was deemed necessary. The surgical site was closed primarily.

The patient underwent a partial glossectomy with selective neck dissection for a well-differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the right lateral tongue. Intraoperatively, the lesion was found to invade the muscular layer but was confined to the tongue without extension to the floor of the mouth. A selective neck dissection of levels I-III was performed, and three of the twenty nodes were positive for metastatic disease. The surgical margins were clear, and the patient tolerated the procedure well with no immediate complications.

A total laryngectomy was performed on a patient with advanced squamous cell carcinoma of the glottis, which had infiltrated the thyroid cartilage and extended into the subglottic region. The procedure included removal of the entire larynx and bilateral modified radical neck dissection. The tumor was noted to have perineural invasion, and the pathology report confirmed the presence of metastatic deposits in two lymph nodes on the right side. The patient required a tracheostomy and will need postoperative radiation therapy.

The patient was taken to the operating room for excision of a T1 lesion on the left buccal mucosa. The lesion was excised with a 1 cm margin of healthy tissue. The surrounding tissue was free of any palpable masses, and no lymphadenopathy was observed in the cervical region. Frozen section analysis confirmed clear margins, and the wound was closed primarily. The patient was discharged on postoperative day one with instructions for follow-up.

The procedure involved a transoral robotic surgery (TORS) to remove a T2 base of tongue carcinoma. The tumor was confined to the base of the tongue and did not extend to the lingual tonsils or the vallecula. The surgery was uneventful, and the tumor was excised with negative margins. No neck dissection was performed as there was no clinical or radiological evidence of nodal metastases. The patient recovered well and was discharged on day two with a liquid diet.

The patient presented with a large ulcerative mass on the left tonsillar fossa, diagnosed as squamous cell carcinoma. A radical tonsillectomy with ipsilateral neck dissection was performed, including levels I-IV. The tumor had invaded the surrounding soft tissue, and multiple lymph nodes were positive for metastatic involvement. Postoperative recovery was complicated by minor bleeding, which was managed conservatively. The patient will be scheduled for adjuvant chemotherapy and radiation.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, presented with a biopsy-confirmed squamous cell carcinoma of the left vocal cord. The tumor was localized to the glottic region with no evidence of metastasis. A partial laryngectomy was performed, preserving the contralateral vocal cord. Intraoperative frozen sections confirmed clear margins, and the procedure was completed without complications. The patient was extubated in the operating room and transferred to recovery in stable condition.

A 54-year-old female with a history of smoking presented with a large, ulcerative mass in the right tonsillar fossa, diagnosed as squamous cell carcinoma. A wide local excision of the oropharyngeal lesion was performed with ipsilateral neck dissection, revealing metastatic involvement of level II and III lymph nodes. The surgical team achieved negative margins, and no complications were reported during the procedure.

The patient, a 70-year-old male, was diagnosed with a T2N0M0 carcinoma of the floor of the mouth. A composite resection including the anterior portion of the mandible was undertaken, followed by reconstruction using a fibula free flap. The surgery was extensive but completed successfully. The patient had an uneventful postoperative recovery and was closely monitored in the surgical ICU.

A 67-year-old female presented with a hypopharyngeal tumor causing significant dysphagia. A transoral robotic-assisted resection of the tumor was performed, which involved the removal of the affected portion of the piriform sinus. Pathology confirmed squamous cell carcinoma with clear surgical margins. No lymph node involvement was detected on preoperative imaging or during surgery.

The patient, a 59-year-old male, presented with a palpable mass in the left neck, initially thought to be a cyst. Fine-needle aspiration revealed metastatic squamous cell carcinoma, with the primary source in the base of the tongue. A combined surgical approach involving base of tongue resection and radical neck dissection was performed. Multiple nodes were positive for carcinoma, confirming the diagnosis of metastatic disease.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, underwent a partial laryngectomy for a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the glottis. The tumor was localized to the right true vocal cord with no evidence of extralaryngeal spread. A frozen section analysis confirmed clear margins. Post-operative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was extubated successfully in the recovery room. No nodal involvement was noted during the procedure.

A 58-year-old female presented with a rapidly enlarging mass in the left oropharynx. A transoral robotic-assisted resection was conducted, targeting a poorly differentiated carcinoma of the base of the tongue. Intraoperative findings confirmed invasion into the adjacent soft tissue. A selective neck dissection was performed, revealing metastatic involvement in level II lymph nodes bilaterally. Post-operative recovery was complicated by mild dysphagia, managed conservatively.

The patient, a 45-year-old male, underwent a wide local excision for a carcinoma located on the floor of the mouth. The squamous cell carcinoma had infiltrated the sublingual gland, necessitating its removal. Margins were clear following a frozen section. No lymphadenopathy was observed intraoperatively. The patient tolerated the procedure well, with no immediate post-operative complications.

A 70-year-old female with a history of tobacco use presented with a mass in the supraglottic region. A total laryngectomy was performed due to the extensive involvement of the supraglottis and paraglottic space. Multiple levels of cervical lymph nodes were dissected, with metastatic carcinoma identified in level III nodes. The patient was discharged with a tracheostomy tube and is being considered for adjunctive radiotherapy.

The patient, a 50-year-old male, underwent a composite resection for an invasive squamous cell carcinoma affecting the right maxillary sinus with extension into the hard palate. A free flap reconstruction was performed to address the defect. Intraoperative findings included perineural invasion, necessitating additional resection of surrounding tissue. The patient experienced minor bleeding post-operatively, which was managed without surgical intervention.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, presented with a persistent sore throat and hoarseness. Upon laryngoscopic examination, a 2 cm exophytic lesion was identified on the right vocal cord. Biopsy confirmed squamous cell carcinoma. The patient underwent a partial laryngectomy with preservation of the opposite vocal cord. Intraoperative frozen section analysis confirmed clear margins. The right neck was also addressed with a selective neck dissection, revealing metastatic involvement of a level II lymph node. The patient tolerated the procedure well with no immediate postoperative complications.

A 55-year-old female presented with a non-healing ulcer on the lateral border of the tongue. Examination and imaging revealed a 1.5 cm lesion extending into the floor of the mouth. A wide local excision of the tongue lesion with partial glossectomy was performed, ensuring clear margins. An ipsilateral supraomohyoid neck dissection was also conducted, which showed no evidence of nodal metastasis. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was discharged on postoperative day three.

The patient, a 70-year-old male, complained of difficulty swallowing and a noticeable mass in the neck. Imaging revealed a 3 cm tumor in the left tonsil with extensive spread to adjacent tissues and multiple enlarged cervical lymph nodes. A radical tonsillectomy with resection of the surrounding tissue was performed, along with a modified radical neck dissection. Pathology confirmed squamous cell carcinoma with metastases to three lymph nodes. The patient was transferred to the ICU for close monitoring post-surgery.

A 48-year-old male with a history of tobacco use presented with a lesion in the left buccal mucosa. Examination revealed a 2.5 cm ulcerative lesion with induration. Excisional biopsy confirmed squamous cell carcinoma. The patient underwent wide local excision of the buccal mucosa and reconstruction using a radial forearm free flap. The procedure was complicated by significant intraoperative bleeding managed with hemostatic agents. Postoperative course was complicated by flap venous congestion, necessitating revision surgery.

A 67-year-old female was referred for evaluation of a painless mass in the left neck. Imaging and fine needle aspiration suggested a left pyriform sinus carcinoma with involvement of regional lymph nodes. The patient underwent total laryngopharyngectomy with bilateral neck dissections. The complex procedure included reconstruction with a pectoralis major myocutaneous flap. Multiple nodes were positive for metastatic disease. The patient was extubated on postoperative day two and began swallow rehabilitation soon after.

The patient presented with a stage II squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. A total laryngectomy was performed with clear margins, followed by bilateral neck dissection to address potential lymphatic spread. During surgery, no obvious metastases were found, and the vocal cords were noted to be extensively involved. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was transferred to the recovery room in stable condition. Post-operative histopathological analysis confirmed the absence of lymph node metastasis.

The patient was diagnosed with a T3N1M0 carcinoma of the base of the tongue. A transoral robotic-assisted resection was conducted to excise the tumor. Intraoperatively, the tumor was found to infiltrate the surrounding musculature, necessitating a partial glossectomy. A concurrent left selective neck dissection was performed, revealing metastatic involvement of level II lymph nodes. The patient recovered without complications and was advised to follow up for adjuvant therapy.

The patient had a recurrent squamous cell carcinoma in the right buccal mucosa, initially treated with radiation therapy. A wide local excision was performed, with reconstruction using a radial forearm free flap. Margins were clear, and no lymphadenopathy was detected intraoperatively. Given the patient's prior treatment history, careful monitoring for potential recurrence was recommended.

This patient presented with a newly diagnosed carcinoma of the hypopharynx, classified as T2N2bM0. The surgical team performed a partial pharyngectomy combined with a right-sided modified radical neck dissection. Multiple lymph nodes showed metastatic carcinoma upon frozen section analysis. Post-operative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was scheduled for adjuvant chemoradiation.

The patient, with a history of smoking, was diagnosed with a squamous cell carcinoma of the nasal cavity. A complete surgical resection was achieved through an endoscopic approach, and the tumor was excised with clear margins. The surgery was uncomplicated, and no lymphatic spread was observed. The patient was discharged with instructions for regular follow-up visits to monitor for any signs of recurrence.

The patient presented with a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the right lateral tongue. A partial glossectomy was performed with clear margins. During the procedure, a selective neck dissection was undertaken to address potential regional metastases, as pre-operative imaging suggested suspicious lymph nodes. Two lymph nodes were pathologically positive for metastatic involvement. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was extubated in the operating room.

A 68-year-old male with a history of smoking presented with hoarseness and difficulty swallowing. A direct laryngoscopy revealed a T3 tumor of the supraglottic larynx. A supracricoid partial laryngectomy was performed. The resection included the bilateral true and false vocal cords, with reconstruction using a cricohyoidoepiglottopexy. No lymph node dissection was performed as imaging showed no evidence of nodal disease.

The patient underwent a transoral robotic resection of a T1 carcinoma of the tonsillar fossa. Margins were clear, and no evidence of extracapsular spread was noted. Due to the early stage of the disease and absence of suspicious lymphadenopathy on imaging, no neck dissection was performed. The patient recovered uneventfully and was discharged on postoperative day two.

A comprehensive bilateral neck dissection was performed on a patient with a known T4a squamous cell carcinoma of the base of the tongue with prior chemoradiotherapy. The surgery involved resection of the primary tumor along with adjacent soft tissues, as well as removal of multiple lymph nodes, among which four were positive for metastasis. The extensive procedure was necessary due to nodal involvement and tumor infiltration into surrounding musculature.

The patient, a 52-year-old female, presented with a non-healing ulcer on the floor of the mouth. Biopsy confirmed a T2 squamous cell carcinoma. Surgical resection included wide local excision with clear margins and a left-sided neck dissection. One of eleven lymph nodes was found to be positive for metastatic disease, confirming the need for additional adjuvant therapy. The patient was stable postoperatively and was discharged with instructions for follow-up care.

The patient underwent a partial laryngectomy due to a squamous cell carcinoma located at the glottic region of the larynx. The tumor was confined to the right vocal cord, with no evidence of extension to the subglottic region or invasion of surrounding structures. During the procedure, careful preservation of the contralateral vocal cord was ensured to maintain postoperative voice function. No lymphadenopathy was noted intraoperatively, and the resection margins were clear on frozen section analysis.

A transoral robotic surgery (TORS) was performed to excise a T2 tumor from the base of the tongue, suspected to be HPV-positive squamous cell carcinoma. The lesion was well-demarcated, and there was no gross invasion into deeper muscular structures. The resection was extended to the ipsilateral tonsillar fossa to ensure clear margins. Concurrently, a selective neck dissection was performed, revealing metastatic involvement of a single ipsilateral level II lymph node.

The patient presented with a large exophytic mass in the left maxillary sinus, diagnosed as an adenoid cystic carcinoma. A maxillectomy was performed, including resection of the posterior and inferior walls of the sinus, with reconstruction using a free radial forearm flap for coverage. The tumor extended to the posterior wall, but there was no invasion into the orbit or cranial base. No lymph node dissection was performed as there was no clinical evidence of nodal metastasis.

Radical neck dissection was performed on a patient with a known carcinoma of the hypopharynx, specifically involving the left piriform sinus. The tumor had invaded the prevertebral fascia, necessitating en bloc resection with the involved cervical lymph nodes. The level III and IV nodes were found to be positive for metastatic disease. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, with plans for adjuvant chemoradiotherapy given the advanced stage of the disease.

A wide local excision of a buccal mucosa lesion was performed, diagnosed as a verrucous carcinoma. The tumor was excised with a 1 cm margin, and intraoperative frozen sections confirmed clear margins. No bony involvement of the mandible was noted, and the patient did not present with any clinically palpable cervical lymphadenopathy. The defect was closed primarily, and the patient was advised on regular follow-up for surveillance.

The patient underwent a partial glossectomy to excise a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma located on the right lateral border of the tongue. The lesion measured approximately 2.5 cm in diameter. Margins were taken and confirmed clear by frozen section analysis. No lymphadenopathy was detected during the procedure, and the surrounding soft tissues appeared uninvolved. Hemostasis was achieved with electrocautery, and the defect was closed primarily with vicryl sutures. Postoperative recovery was uneventful.

The patient presented with a T2N1M0 squamous cell carcinoma of the supraglottic larynx. A total laryngectomy was performed with bilateral selective neck dissection to address metastatic spread to the ipsilateral level II lymph nodes. The primary tumor was excised with clear margins, and two of the excised lymph nodes tested positive for metastasis. A tracheoesophageal puncture was created for voice rehabilitation. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was extubated without complications.

In a case of advanced oropharyngeal carcinoma, the patient underwent a radical resection of the tumor involving the base of the tongue and extending into the oropharyngeal wall. The tumor was measured at 4 cm with invasion into the adjacent soft tissue. A left modified radical neck dissection was also performed due to palpable lymph nodes, which were confirmed metastatic in nature. Reconstruction was achieved using a radial forearm free flap. The patient was stable postoperatively, with no immediate complications.

The patient was diagnosed with a small, well-differentiated carcinoma on the floor of the mouth. A wide local excision was performed, successfully removing the 1.5 cm lesion with adequate margins. Intraoperative assessment showed no involvement of the submandibular gland or the nearby neurovascular structures. A sentinel lymph node biopsy was negative for metastasis. The surgical site was closed in layers, and the patient was transferred to recovery in stable condition.

A patient with hypopharyngeal carcinoma underwent a combined pharyngolaryngectomy and esophagectomy due to extensive tumor infiltration into the cervical esophagus. The primary tumor was 6 cm in size, with extraluminal extension. A gastric pull-up was performed for reconstruction of the esophageal continuity. The procedure included bilateral neck dissections, revealing metastatic involvement in multiple lymph nodes on the right side. The patient was admitted to the intensive care unit for postoperative monitoring.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, presented with a two-month history of hoarseness and difficulty swallowing. Laryngoscopy revealed a mass located on the left true vocal cord. A biopsy confirmed squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. During the surgical procedure, a partial laryngectomy was performed with preservation of the contralateral vocal cord. No lymph node involvement was detected. The tumor was completely excised with clear margins, and the patient was extubated without complications.

A 54-year-old female was admitted for surgical management of a known oropharyngeal carcinoma. Imaging studies showed a primary tumor in the base of the tongue with ipsilateral level II lymphadenopathy. The patient underwent a transoral robotic surgery for resection of the oropharyngeal tumor followed by a left neck dissection. Pathology confirmed a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma with metastatic involvement of two lymph nodes.

A 47-year-old male presented with a rapidly enlarging mass in the right mandibular region. CT scans revealed a large tumor involving the right floor of the mouth and extending to the mandibular bone. The patient underwent a composite resection including partial mandibulectomy and resection of the floor of the mouth. Reconstruction was performed with a fibula free flap. The pathology report confirmed an invasive squamous cell carcinoma. No lymph nodes were involved.

A 70-year-old male with a history of smoking presented with a persistent sore throat and otalgia. Examination and imaging identified a mass in the posterior wall of the hypopharynx. The patient underwent a total laryngopharyngectomy with bilateral neck dissection. Multiple bilateral cervical lymph nodes were involved upon pathological examination. The surgical procedure was successful with clear margins achieved.

The patient, a 65-year-old female, was evaluated for a lesion in her right maxillary sinus, causing nasal obstruction and facial swelling. Biopsy confirmed squamous cell carcinoma. A maxillectomy was performed, including the resection of the involved sinus, and reconstruction with an obturator was achieved. Post-operative recovery was uneventful. No regional lymph node metastases were identified.

The patient, a 58-year-old male, presented with a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the right lateral tongue. The procedure involved a partial glossectomy with clear margins achieved. Intraoperative frozen sections confirmed negative margins. A selective neck dissection was performed, removing levels I-III lymph nodes, which were positive for metastatic involvement. Reconstruction was accomplished using a radial forearm free flap, ensuring functional and aesthetic outcomes.

A 72-year-old female with a history of smoking and alcohol use underwent a total laryngectomy for a T4a laryngeal carcinoma involving the left vocal cord and extending to the subglottic region. The procedure included resection of the thyroid cartilage and removal of the pre-epiglottic space. Bilateral neck dissection was performed, with metastases identified in the left level IV lymph nodes. A tracheoesophageal puncture was created for voice rehabilitation.

The patient, a 65-year-old male, was diagnosed with a T1 squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx located at the base of the tongue. A transoral robotic surgery (TORS) approach was utilized to excise the tumor with adequate margins. No lymphadenopathy was noted, and no neck dissection was performed. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was discharged on postoperative day two.

A 60-year-old female underwent a composite resection for a T3 carcinoma of the left maxillary sinus, including a partial maxillectomy and resection of the involved hard palate. The surgery extended to the orbital floor, which was reconstructed using a titanium mesh. Bilateral level I-III neck dissection was performed, revealing no metastatic lymph nodes. The defect was covered using a temporalis muscle flap.

The patient, a 55-year-old male, underwent a radical resection for a T3 hypopharyngeal carcinoma with invasion into the cervical esophagus. The surgery included a total pharyngolaryngectomy and cervical esophagectomy. A gastric pull-up was performed to restore alimentary continuity. Bilateral neck dissection was conducted, with involvement found in the right level II and III nodes. Postoperative recovery was complicated by transient dysphagia.

The patient underwent a partial laryngectomy for a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the left vocal cord. The procedure involved careful resection of the tumor with preservation of surrounding healthy tissue to maintain as much function as possible. Intraoperative findings confirmed the tumor was confined to the vocal cord without extension into the surrounding structures. Margins were negative for malignant cells. The surgery was completed without complications, and the patient was stable in recovery.

A 62-year-old male patient underwent a composite resection for an invasive carcinoma of the floor of the mouth. The surgery included resection of the tumor with part of the mandible and adjacent soft tissues. A modified radical neck dissection was conducted on the right side due to palpable lymph nodes. Pathological examination revealed metastatic involvement of the cervical lymph nodes. Reconstruction was achieved using a fibular free flap. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was transferred to the ICU for monitoring.

Surgery was performed on a 55-year-old female presenting with a carcinoma of the left tonsil. The procedure was a transoral robotic surgery (TORS) to excise the tumor, which was confined to the tonsillar region. In addition, a selective neck dissection was carried out to remove potentially affected lymph nodes. The pathological assessment indicated clear margins and no evidence of metastasis in the removed nodes. The patient recovered well postoperatively and was discharged on postoperative day two.

The patient had a resection for a squamous cell carcinoma located in the hypopharynx, specifically at the pyriform sinus. Due to the advanced stage of the tumor, a total laryngopharyngectomy was performed, accompanied by bilateral neck dissection. The procedure was extensive, given the tumor's infiltration into the surrounding tissues and the presence of bilateral lymph node metastases. Postoperative care involved close monitoring in the ICU, and the patient is planned for adjuvant therapy.

An 80-year-old male underwent an excisional biopsy of a suspicious lesion on the buccal mucosa. The tumor was localized, and a wide local excision was performed, ensuring clear margins. During the procedure, the surrounding tissues were carefully preserved, and no lymphadenopathy was noted on palpation. The histopathological report confirmed a well-differentiated squamous cell carcinoma with no further invasive characteristics.

The patient underwent a total laryngectomy with bilateral neck dissection due to an invasive squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. The tumor was staged at T3N2bM0, indicating significant local extension and contralateral lymph node involvement. The procedure involved the complete removal of the larynx, preservation of the thyroid gland, and meticulous dissection of the cervical lymph nodes. Intraoperative findings revealed multiple enlarged lymph nodes, particularly in levels II and III of the right neck, which were successfully excised. Post-surgical pathology confirmed metastatic spread to these nodes.

The patient presented with a rapidly enlarging mass in the left base of the tongue, which was confirmed to be a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx. A transoral robotic surgery (TORS) was performed to excise the primary lesion, followed by selective neck dissection to address clinically suspicious lymph nodes in the ipsilateral neck. The surgical team ensured clear margins were achieved, and two of the excised nodes were positive for metastatic disease on frozen section analysis.

The patient had a history of tobacco use and presented with a non-healing ulcer on the right buccal mucosa. A biopsy confirmed the presence of a T1N0M0 squamous cell carcinoma. Wide local excision of the lesion with 1 cm margins was performed, along with primary closure of the defect. No lymphadenopathy was noted on imaging or during surgery, and the lymphatic basin was not addressed. Pathological examination later confirmed clear margins with no perineural invasion.

The patient was diagnosed with an advanced hypopharyngeal carcinoma, staged T4aN1M0, with the tumor infiltrating the prevertebral fascia. A combined approach involving a total pharyngolaryngectomy and reconstruction with a radial forearm free flap was undertaken. The procedure included unilateral neck dissection on the left side, where the solitary metastatic lymph node was found. The patient had a tracheostomy placed post-operatively for airway management.

The patient underwent a partial glossectomy for a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the anterior two-thirds of the tongue. The surgical plan included resection of the tumor with reconstruction using a local myocutaneous flap. Intraoperative frozen sections confirmed negative margins. Given the absence of clinically apparent lymphadenopathy and the early stage of the tumor, a sentinel lymph node biopsy was not performed.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, underwent a partial laryngectomy for a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma located on the left true vocal cord. The tumor measured 2.5 cm in greatest dimension and involved the anterior commissure. A selective neck dissection was performed, revealing metastatic involvement in the ipsilateral level II lymph nodes. Hemostasis was achieved, and the patient was extubated without complication.

A 55-year-old female presented with a 3 cm exophytic mass on the left tonsil. A transoral robotic-assisted resection was performed, and the pathology confirmed poorly differentiated carcinoma. The procedure included a reconstructive pharyngoplasty using a radial forearm free flap. Lymph node dissection revealed no metastatic spread. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was transferred to the recovery unit in stable condition.

The patient, a 70-year-old male with a history of tobacco use, presented with a 4 cm ulcerative lesion on the right lateral border of the tongue. A wide local excision was performed, including a marginal mandibulectomy. The histopathological examination confirmed invasive squamous cell carcinoma. Submandibular gland excision was also performed due to tumor proximity, and no lymph node metastasis was detected in the level IB nodes.

A 48-year-old male underwent a total glossectomy for a large, infiltrative squamous cell carcinoma involving the base of the tongue. The tumor extended into the vallecula and had bilateral cervical lymph node involvement. A bilateral neck dissection was performed, and multiple nodes were positive for metastatic carcinoma. Tracheostomy was performed for airway management. The patient is planned for adjuvant chemoradiotherapy.

A 60-year-old female presented with dysphagia and was found to have a 2 cm lesion in the hypopharynx upon endoscopic examination. The lesion was located at the piriform sinus. A trans-cervical approach was used for excision, and the pathology revealed moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma. No lymphovascular invasion was observed, and the surgical margins were clear. The postoperative course was uneventful.

The patient underwent a partial laryngectomy for a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx, located on the right vocal cord. The surgery was performed under general anesthesia. Intraoperative findings revealed a well-defined tumor with no extralaryngeal extension. The resection was carried out with preservation of the contralateral vocal cord. Margins were confirmed to be clear of tumor involvement via frozen section analysis. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was extubated in the operating room. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, with good voice preservation noted.

The patient presented with a locally advanced, ulcerative lesion on the left lateral tongue, diagnosed as squamous cell carcinoma. A wide local excision was performed, along with a left neck dissection due to the presence of clinically positive cervical lymph nodes. The tongue defect was reconstructed using a radial forearm free flap. Intraoperative assessment confirmed the complete excision of the tumor with clear margins. Two out of fifteen dissected lymph nodes were positive for metastatic disease. The patient was transferred to the recovery unit in stable condition.

A 54-year-old male underwent a transoral robotic surgery (TORS) for a T1 carcinoma of the base of the tongue. The tumor was confined to the mucosa and measured 1.5 cm. Clear margins were achieved, as confirmed by frozen section. No neck dissection was deemed necessary given the early stage of the tumor and absence of palpable lymph nodes. The patient experienced minimal blood loss and was extubated without complications. Postoperative swallowing function was preserved.

The surgical team performed a composite resection for an extensive squamous cell carcinoma involving the right maxillary sinus and hard palate. The resection included the maxilla, part of the hard palate, and surrounding soft tissues. A free fibula flap was used for reconstruction of the osseous defect. The intraoperative findings demonstrated invasion into the nasal cavity but no orbital or intracranial extension. Postoperative histopathology confirmed clear margins. The patient was stable postoperatively and transferred to the intensive care unit for monitoring.

The patient underwent a total thyroidectomy for a poorly differentiated thyroid carcinoma with invasion into adjacent structures, including the trachea and esophagus. During the procedure, the recurrent laryngeal nerves were identified and preserved bilaterally. Postoperatively, the patient required a tracheostomy due to significant airway compromise from tumor invasion and edema. The patient was monitored in the intensive care unit, and plans for adjuvant therapy were discussed with oncology.

The patient underwent a partial glossectomy for a T2 squamous cell carcinoma located at the lateral border of the tongue. The tumor extended through the muscular layer but did not invade the adjacent floor of the mouth. Margins were negative for carcinoma. A selective neck dissection was performed, revealing metastatic involvement in one out of fifteen lymph nodes in Level II. Post-operative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was discharged on post-operative day five with recommendations for adjuvant radiotherapy.

A total laryngectomy was performed on a patient with a T4a squamous cell carcinoma of the glottis. The tumor invaded the thyroid cartilage and extended to the soft tissues of the neck. Bilateral neck dissections were conducted, with metastatic carcinoma found in the left Level III lymph nodes. The patient also had a tracheoesophageal puncture for voice rehabilitation. The procedure was completed without complications, and the patient will begin speech therapy post-discharge.

The patient presented with a T1 carcinoma of the right tonsil, managed through a transoral robotic surgery (TORS). The primary tumor was completely excised with clear margins, and no evidence of extracapsular spread was noted. No neck dissection was performed due to the early stage and absence of clinically palpable lymphadenopathy. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was discharged the following day with a follow-up in two weeks.

A radical neck dissection was performed in conjunction with a wide local excision for a T3 carcinoma of the hypopharynx. The lesion involved the piriform sinus and extended to the post-cricoid region. Metastatic spread was confirmed in three out of twenty lymph nodes, with extracapsular extension noted in one node. The patient required a temporary tracheostomy, which will be assessed for decannulation after the acute post-operative period.

An anterior maxillectomy was conducted for a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the hard palate. The tumor had invaded the maxillary sinus, necessitating a partial resection of the sinus wall. Reconstruction was achieved using a palatal obturator. No lymph node dissection was performed as there was no clinical or radiological evidence of nodal metastasis. The patient recovered without complications and was discharged with instructions for prosthetic adjustments and follow-up.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, presented with a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the left vocal cord. The lesion was resected via a partial laryngectomy. Intraoperative findings confirmed the tumor was confined to the left true vocal cord, with clear margins achieved. The procedure was uncomplicated, and the patient was extubated successfully postoperatively. No evidence of lymph node involvement was noted during the surgery, and the pathology report confirmed no metastatic spread.

A 58-year-old female with a history of smoking presented with a T3 tumor of the right tonsil, extending to the base of the tongue. The surgical procedure involved a transoral robotic-assisted radical tonsillectomy with partial glossectomy. Multiple enlarged cervical lymph nodes were identified and excised, confirming metastatic involvement. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was referred for adjuvant radiation therapy.

The patient, a 45-year-old male, was diagnosed with a T1 verrucous carcinoma located on the floor of the mouth. A wide local excision was performed, ensuring a 1 cm margin around the tumor. The surgical site was reconstructed using a split-thickness skin graft harvested from the thigh. Intraoperative frozen sections were negative for malignancy at the margins, and no lymph node dissection was needed. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was discharged on postoperative day two.

A 70-year-old male presented with a T4a squamous cell carcinoma of the hypopharynx, involving the piriform sinus and extending into adjacent structures. A total laryngopharyngectomy was performed with bilateral neck dissection. Intraoperative assessment revealed multiple involved lymph nodes on the right side; these were resected and confirmed as metastatic. The patient was placed on a nasogastric tube for postoperative feeding and is planned for intensive rehabilitation and adjuvant chemoradiotherapy.

The patient, a 53-year-old female, underwent excision of a T2 adenocarcinoma of the minor salivary gland located in the hard palate. The procedure involved a wide local excision with removal of the underlying periosteum. Reconstruction was achieved using a palatal obturator for immediate postoperative support. No lymphadenopathy was appreciated, and the pathology report confirmed clear surgical margins with no evidence of metastatic disease.

The patient underwent a partial laryngectomy to excise a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma located on the right true vocal cord. The procedure included the removal of the superior portion of the thyroid cartilage. An intraoperative frozen section confirmed negative margins. The right recurrent laryngeal nerve was preserved, and no evidence of extralaryngeal spread was observed. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, with the patient extubated without difficulty and transferred to the surgical ward for monitoring.

The patient presented with a large, ulcerative lesion on the left lateral tongue, diagnosed as invasive squamous cell carcinoma. A hemiglossectomy was performed, and the defect was reconstructed using a radial forearm free flap. Additionally, a selective neck dissection was conducted, revealing metastatic involvement in two of the left level II lymph nodes. Postoperative recovery was complicated by temporary dysphagia, managed with a nasogastric feeding tube.

The patient, with a history of tobacco use, underwent a transoral robotic resection of a T2 oropharyngeal carcinoma located at the base of the tongue. The procedure was successful with clear margins, and bilateral tonsillectomy was also performed to ensure complete removal of potential local spread. No lymph node involvement was detected during the procedure, and the patient was discharged with instructions for close follow-up in the oncology clinic.

A total laryngopharyngectomy was performed on a patient with advanced hypopharyngeal carcinoma causing significant airway obstruction. The tumor extended to the piriform sinus and involved the cricoid cartilage. A comprehensive bilateral neck dissection was included, and several lymph nodes were positive for metastasis. Reconstruction was achieved using a gastric pull-up procedure. The patient was transferred to the ICU for postoperative care and monitoring of vital signs and respiratory status.

The patient underwent maxillectomy for a squamous cell carcinoma of the hard palate, with invasion into the maxillary sinus. Reconstruction was performed using a maxillary obturator. The surgery included an ipsilateral neck dissection, which revealed no metastatic lymph nodes. Postoperative recovery included management of oroantral fistula and fitting of the prosthetic obturator to facilitate speech and swallowing.

The patient, a 64-year-old male, presented with a squamous cell carcinoma of the left lateral tongue. A partial glossectomy was performed, with clear margins achieved. Intraoperative frozen sections confirmed the absence of malignancy at the resection edges. Given the presence of a 2 cm metastatic lymph node in level II of the left neck, a selective neck dissection was conducted. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was advised to begin adjuvant radiotherapy.

A 58-year-old female patient was diagnosed with a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx, specifically involving the glottic region. A total laryngectomy was performed due to extensive involvement of the vocal cords and impaired airway function. The surgery included the creation of a tracheostomy and the removal of bilateral level III lymph nodes, which were negative for metastasis. The patient will require speech rehabilitation and follow-up imaging to monitor for recurrence.

The case involved a 72-year-old male with a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the right tonsil. A transoral robotic surgery (TORS) was performed to excise the tumor with adequate margins. Additionally, due to the suspicious lymphadenopathy noted on preoperative imaging, a right-sided modified radical neck dissection was executed, revealing metastatic involvement in levels II and III. The postoperative course was complicated by mild dysphagia, which is expected to improve with therapy.

A 45-year-old female presented with a small, superficial carcinoma of the floor of the mouth, measuring 1.5 cm. A wide local excision was undertaken, with frozen section analysis confirming negative margins. No cervical lymphadenopathy was detected on imaging or during surgery, thus no neck dissection was performed. The patient recovered without complications and was scheduled for regular follow-up visits.

The patient, a 67-year-old male, had an advanced carcinoma involving the hypopharynx with extension into the cervical esophagus. A total pharyngolaryngectomy with esophageal reconstruction was carried out. A bilateral neck dissection was performed, with pathological examination of the lymph nodes revealing metastatic disease on the left side. The patient was monitored in the intensive care unit postoperatively and required a feeding tube for nutritional support.

The patient underwent a partial laryngectomy due to a T2 squamous cell carcinoma located on the true vocal cords. The lesion measured 2.5 cm and was resected with clear margins. Intraoperative frozen section analysis was performed to ensure negative margins. The surgery was uneventful, and the patient was extubated without difficulty. Postoperative laryngoscopy confirmed the successful preservation of the laryngeal function. The histopathological examination revealed well-differentiated carcinoma with no perineural invasion or lymphovascular spread.

A transoral robotic surgery (TORS) was performed for a patient presenting with a T1 carcinoma of the left tonsil. The tumor measured 1.2 cm and was confined to the oropharynx without breaching the tonsillar capsule. The surrounding tissues were meticulously dissected to achieve clear margins while preserving the surrounding structures. The procedure was complemented by a selective neck dissection of levels II-IV on the left side due to suspicious lymph nodes. Pathology confirmed metastatic involvement in one of the six lymph nodes.

The patient presented with a T3 poorly differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the hypopharynx, invading the piriform sinus and extending to the cervical esophagus. A total laryngopharyngectomy was performed, along with a bilateral neck dissection. Reconstruction was achieved using a free jejunal graft for esophageal continuity. Intraoperative findings indicated extensive lymph node involvement with extracapsular spread on the right side. Postoperative recovery was complicated by minor anastomotic leakage, which was managed conservatively.

A wide local excision was conducted for a T1 squamous cell carcinoma located on the floor of the mouth, measuring 0.8 cm. The lesion was excised with 1 cm margins, and the defect was primarily closed. A sentinel lymph node biopsy was performed, revealing no evidence of metastatic disease. Frozen sections confirmed clear margins, and the patient tolerated the procedure well, with no postoperative complications. Follow-up plans include close surveillance and consideration of adjuvant therapy if necessary.

The patient underwent a radical neck dissection following the diagnosis of a T4a squamous cell carcinoma of the tongue base, with extensive infiltration into the surrounding musculature and deep tissue planes. The procedure included resection of the primary tumor and involved ipsilateral neck lymph nodes. Reconstruction was performed using a radial forearm free flap to restore the defect at the base of the tongue. Pathological examination indicated poor differentiation and perineural invasion, with metastatic involvement of multiple cervical lymph nodes.

The patient, a 56-year-old male, presented with a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the left true vocal cord. A transoral laser microsurgery was performed to excise the tumor with clear margins. Intraoperative frozen section analysis confirmed the absence of residual tumor. Post-operative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was extubated without complications. No lymphatic spread was noted, and the surrounding tissues appeared uninvolved.

A 62-year-old female was diagnosed with a T3 carcinoma of the right tonsil with ipsilateral neck node metastasis. The surgical procedure included a radical tonsillectomy followed by a modified radical neck dissection on the right side. Multiple lymph nodes were excised, and histopathology revealed metastasis in two of the six nodes removed. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was transferred to recovery in stable condition.

The 49-year-old male patient had a T4a squamous cell carcinoma of the oral tongue with extensive infiltration into the floor of the mouth. A composite resection involving partial glossectomy and floor of mouth resection was performed. A concurrent bilateral neck dissection revealed metastatic involvement in the cervical lymph nodes bilaterally. The reconstruction was achieved using a radial forearm free flap. Post-operatively, the patient experienced mild edema but was otherwise stable.

A 70-year-old male presented with a T1 glottic carcinoma. A minimally invasive endoscopic resection was performed, removing the tumor in its entirety. The surrounding laryngeal structures were intact, with no evidence of lymph node involvement. The patient was discharged the following day with instructions for voice rest and follow-up in two weeks.

The patient, a 66-year-old female, was found to have a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the hypopharynx, with suspicious enlargement of lymph nodes in the left neck. A partial pharyngectomy was performed, along with a selective neck dissection. Pathology confirmed metastasis in one of the excised nodes. The patient was monitored closely post-operatively and demonstrated satisfactory recovery without complications.

The patient underwent a total laryngectomy for a T3 squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. The surgical procedure included the excision of the entire larynx, with preservation of the pharyngeal mucosa. Neck dissection was performed bilaterally to assess potential lymphatic spread. Intraoperative findings confirmed enlarged level II and III nodes on the right side, which were excised and sent for pathological evaluation. The surgery was complicated by significant intraoperative bleeding, which was controlled successfully. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was transferred to the intensive care unit for monitoring.

A 56-year-old female presented with a mass in the left tonsillar region, diagnosed as squamous cell carcinoma. The patient underwent a transoral robotic surgery (TORS) for tumor resection, with clear margins achieved. A selective neck dissection was also performed on the ipsilateral side to remove level I and II lymph nodes. The procedure was completed without complications, and the patient was extubated in the operating room. No evidence of distant metastasis was noted on preoperative imaging.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, was admitted for resection of a T2 carcinoma of the floor of the mouth. The surgery involved wide local excision of the tumor with a 1 cm margin of normal tissue. Reconstruction was performed using a radial forearm free flap to restore the defect. No lymph node involvement was detected during the dissection, and the procedure was completed without any intraoperative complications. The patient was closely monitored in the recovery room and showed satisfactory recovery.

A 49-year-old male presented with a mass at the base of the tongue. Biopsy confirmed invasive squamous cell carcinoma. The patient underwent a partial glossectomy with bilateral neck dissection. Level II and III nodes were removed and sent for pathological analysis. The surgery was complex due to the proximity of the tumor to the lingual artery, which was carefully preserved. Postoperative imaging showed no residual tumor, and the patient was initiated on a liquid diet when stable.

The patient, a 71-year-old female, underwent a radical neck dissection for an advanced hypopharyngeal carcinoma. The tumor involved the piriform sinus and extended to the posterior pharyngeal wall. A total pharyngolaryngectomy was performed, with removal of the surrounding affected tissues. Reconstruction was achieved using an anterolateral thigh flap. Intraoperative frozen sections confirmed negative margins. Postoperative care included a tracheostomy for airway management, and the patient was transferred to the surgical ward in stable condition.

The patient underwent a total laryngectomy with bilateral neck dissection due to a T3 squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. The tumor involved the right vocal cord and extended to the subglottic region but did not invade the thyroid cartilage. Bilateral level II-IV lymph nodes were dissected, revealing metastatic involvement on the right side. The surgery was uneventful, and the patient was transferred to the recovery unit in stable condition.

A partial glossectomy was performed on a patient presenting with a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the tongue, located on the left lateral border. The tumor was excised with a 1 cm margin, and frozen sections confirmed clear margins. The left neck was also addressed with a selective neck dissection of levels I-III, which showed no evidence of metastatic disease. The patient's postoperative course was smooth, and they were advised on speech and swallowing therapy.

The patient presented with a T4a carcinoma of the left maxillary sinus, with invasion into the oral cavity. A maxillectomy was performed, along with a reconstruction using a free fibula flap. The tumor had extended into the hard palate, necessitating its partial removal. No lymph node involvement was noted in the ipsilateral selective neck dissection. The reconstructive phase was successful, and the patient was extubated postoperatively without complications.

A patient with a history of smoking underwent a wide local excision of a T1 squamous cell carcinoma of the right tonsil. The procedure included a marginal mandibulectomy to ensure clear anterior margins due to the proximity of the tumor to the mandible. No neck dissection was performed as imaging showed no evidence of nodal disease. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was discharged with instructions for rigorous oral hygiene and follow-up.

The patient with a T2 carcinoma of the hypopharynx underwent a partial pharyngectomy and neck dissection. The tumor was located in the piriform sinus, and a segmental resection was performed. Level II and III lymph nodes on the left side were positive for metastatic involvement. Postoperative recovery was complicated by a minor pharyngocutaneous fistula, which was managed conservatively.

The patient presented with a locally advanced squamous cell carcinoma of the right larynx, involving the vocal cords and extending into the subglottic region. A total laryngectomy was performed, followed by bilateral neck dissections due to suspected lymph node involvement. Intraoperative findings confirmed multiple metastatic nodes in the right cervical region. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, with the patient scheduled for adjuvant radiotherapy.

The patient, diagnosed with a moderately differentiated carcinoma of the left tonsil, underwent a transoral robotic surgery (TORS) for resection of the primary oropharyngeal tumor. Margins were clear, and no lymphovascular invasion was observed. A selective neck dissection was performed, revealing no metastatic lymph node involvement. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was discharged on postoperative day three.

A 67-year-old male with a history of smoking presented with a T2 lesion of the floor of the mouth. A wide local excision was performed with the reconstruction of the defect using a radial forearm free flap. The operation included a selective neck dissection on the left side, which revealed two positive lymph nodes. The patient is recovering well and will be evaluated for potential adjuvant chemotherapy.

The patient presented with a primary carcinoma of the hypopharynx, specifically in the pyriform sinus, with significant dysphagia. A total pharyngolaryngectomy with the creation of a permanent tracheostomy was undertaken. Preoperative imaging had suggested possible regional lymph node metastases, confirmed intraoperatively in the left neck. The patient is expected to begin speech therapy post-recovery.

The patient was diagnosed with a small, early-stage carcinoma on the lower lip. A wide local excision was performed with primary closure achieved. Sentinel lymph node biopsy was negative for metastatic disease. The surgery was uncomplicated, and the patient was discharged on the same day with instructions for careful wound care.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, presented with a squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx, predominantly affecting the glottic region. The surgical team performed a partial laryngectomy, preserving the patient's vocal cords. Intraoperative findings confirmed the tumor was confined to the larynx with no evidence of invasion beyond the thyroid cartilage. Lymph nodes in the neck were palpated and appeared non-enlarged, suggesting no metastatic spread. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was transferred to recovery in stable condition.

A 55-year-old female was diagnosed with a malignant neoplasm of the left tonsil. The tumor was noted to have invaded the surrounding oropharyngeal tissue, necessitating a radical tonsillectomy along with a partial resection of the soft palate. Due to suspicious cervical lymphadenopathy, a left-sided neck dissection was also performed. Histopathology later confirmed metastatic involvement in two of the dissected lymph nodes. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was referred for adjuvant radiotherapy.

The patient, a 70-year-old male, underwent surgery for a carcinoma of the floor of the mouth. The lesion was extensive, requiring a composite resection involving part of the mandible and adjacent soft tissues. Margins were carefully evaluated intraoperatively and confirmed to be free of tumor invasion. No lymphadenopathy was evident on examination or imaging prior to surgery. The patient was fitted with a temporary obturator to assist with postoperative oral functions.

A 48-year-old male presented with a mass in the left piriform sinus, identified as a squamous cell carcinoma on biopsy. The patient underwent a total laryngopharyngectomy with reconstruction using a radial forearm free flap. Multiple enlarged cervical lymph nodes were noted and a bilateral neck dissection was performed. Pathological examination revealed metastatic spread to several lymph nodes. Postoperatively, the patient was monitored in the ICU and later transitioned to the oncology unit for further care.

The patient, a 65-year-old female, presented with a lesion on the hard palate, which biopsy confirmed as malignant. The surgical approach involved a maxillectomy, sparing the orbital floor. Reconstruction was achieved using a titanium mesh and a local flap. There was no clinical or radiological evidence of nodal metastasis. The procedure was completed successfully, and the patient was referred to the maxillofacial rehabilitation team for further reconstructive planning.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, presented with a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. A total laryngectomy was performed, involving the complete removal of the larynx and surrounding tissues. The surgical margins were clear of carcinoma. Bilateral neck dissection was executed due to suspected lymphatic spread, revealing involvement of two lymph nodes on the left side. Post-operative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was scheduled for adjuvant radiotherapy.

A 57-year-old female was diagnosed with a T1 carcinoma of the floor of the mouth. The patient underwent transoral excision of the tumor with laser ablation. The surgical resection was successful, with negative margins confirmed by frozen section analysis. No lymph node involvement was noted upon examination, and the patient was deemed to have a low risk of recurrence. The patient was discharged with instructions for regular follow-up care.

A 70-year-old male with a history of tobacco use presented with a T3 invasive squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx, specifically affecting the base of the tongue. A radical resection was performed, including partial glossectomy and bilateral neck dissection. Pathological assessment showed metastatic spread to five cervical lymph nodes. Reconstruction was achieved using a radial forearm free flap. The patient was referred for chemotherapy and radiation therapy post-surgery.

The patient, a 65-year-old non-smoking female, was diagnosed with a T4a adenocarcinoma of the nasopharynx. Surgical intervention included an endoscopic resection of the tumor, with additional resection of the posterior ethmoid sinus. Intraoperative findings confirmed the involvement of the base of the skull, necessitating a more extensive resection. The patient was advised to undergo proton beam therapy for better local control of the disease.

A 58-year-old male presented with a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the hypopharynx. The patient underwent a partial pharyngectomy with reconstruction using a pectoralis major myocutaneous flap. The surgical team also performed a selective neck dissection, which revealed one positive lymph node for metastasis. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was subsequently enrolled in a combined modality treatment program involving radiation therapy and cisplatin-based chemotherapy.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, underwent a partial laryngectomy for a T2 carcinoma of the true vocal cord. Intraoperative findings included a well-circumscribed tumor on the left vocal fold, with no invasion into the surrounding laryngeal structures. Margins were assessed intraoperatively and appeared clear. No lymphatic spread was noted, and the preoperative imaging suggested no nodal involvement. The procedure was uneventful, and the patient was extubated in the operating room and transferred to recovery in stable condition.

A 55-year-old female presented with a squamous cell carcinoma of the base of the tongue. The patient underwent a transoral robotic surgery (TORS) for resection of the primary tumor. The tumor was extensive, involving the lateral and posterior aspects of the tongue base, with noted extension into the ipsilateral tonsillar fossa. A selective neck dissection was performed due to palpable lymphadenopathy, and two lymph nodes were positive for metastases. Careful reconstruction of the oropharyngeal defect was achieved with a radial forearm free flap.

The patient, a 67-year-old male, underwent a composite resection for an advanced squamous cell carcinoma of the floor of the mouth. The tumor involved the anterior mandible, requiring segmental mandibulectomy. Intraoperative frozen section analysis confirmed negative margins. A modified radical neck dissection was also performed due to clinical and radiological evidence of nodal metastasis, with three metastatic nodes confirmed. Reconstruction was carried out using a fibular free flap.

A 45-year-old male with a diagnosis of T1 squamous cell carcinoma of the soft palate underwent surgical excision with clear margins. The lesion was confined to the mucosal surface with no invasion into the adjacent structures. No lymph node involvement was detected on preoperative imaging, and intraoperative palpation revealed no abnormal nodes. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was discharged home on postoperative day two with instructions for follow-up.

The patient, a 70-year-old female, was treated for a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the hypopharynx. The tumor was located primarily in the piriform sinus with extension into the postcricoid area. A total laryngopharyngectomy was performed, and bilateral neck dissection was undertaken due to multiple enlarged nodes. Pathological examination confirmed metastatic involvement of four lymph nodes on the right and two on the left. Reconstruction was done using a jejunal free flap, and the patient was transferred to the ICU postoperatively.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, presented with a T2 lesion on the right lateral border of the tongue. A partial glossectomy was performed, ensuring clear margins were achieved. Intraoperative frozen sections confirmed no involvement of the deep muscular tissue. The surgical site was closed using absorbable sutures. Post-operative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was advised on a soft diet to minimize irritation during the healing process.

A 55-year-old female with a history of smoking presented with a carcinoma of the supraglottic larynx. A total laryngectomy was conducted, including the resection of the pre-epiglottic space. Bilateral neck dissection revealed metastatic involvement of level II and III lymph nodes on the left side. A tracheostomy was performed to secure the airway. The patient will require adjuvant radiation therapy given the lymph node involvement.

The patient, a 70-year-old male, was diagnosed with squamous cell carcinoma of the left tonsil. A transoral robotic surgery was performed to excise the tumor, and the margins were confirmed clear by frozen section analysis.
