Left neck dissection was carried out due to clinical suspicion of nodal involvement, but pathology later confirmed the nodes were negative for metastasis. The patient’s recovery was complicated by mild dysphagia, managed conservatively with speech therapy.

A 48-year-old female presented with a suspicious mass in the right maxillary sinus. Imaging confirmed an invasive tumor, and a maxillectomy was performed. The tumor had eroded into the nasal cavity, necessitating reconstruction with a radial forearm free flap to restore functionality and aesthetics. The patient’s post-operative course was smooth, and she was discharged with follow-up appointments scheduled to monitor for recurrence.

The patient is a 66-year-old male with a carcinoma located at the base of the tongue extending to the vallecula. A combined approach with transoral laser microsurgery and selective neck dissection was undertaken. The tumor was excised with negative margins, and pathological examination showed metastatic spread to the ipsilateral level II lymph nodes. The patient was counseled on the need for adjuvant chemoradiation therapy due to nodal involvement.

The patient underwent a partial laryngectomy due to a malignant neoplasm of the glottic region of the larynx. During the procedure, a horizontal incision was made, and the tumor mass was excised with a clear margin. The right vocal cord was involved, but no extension into the subglottic space was observed. Frozen sections confirmed negative margins. No lymphadenopathy was noted upon exploration, and no neck dissection was deemed necessary. The airway was secured with a temporary tracheostomy, which was placed for postoperative airway management.

The patient presented with a squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx, specifically in the tonsillar fossa. A transoral robotic-assisted resection was performed to remove the primary tumor. The procedure included a bilateral selective neck dissection due to suspected lymph node involvement. Multiple enlarged lymph nodes in levels II and III were excised and sent for pathological examination, which confirmed metastatic disease. The tumor was found to infiltrate the surrounding soft tissues, and postoperative radiotherapy was planned.

The patient, diagnosed with a cancerous lesion on the floor of the mouth, underwent a wide local excision with a marginal mandibulectomy. The surgical team carefully preserved the lingual nerve to maintain tongue function. A radial forearm free flap was used for reconstruction of the defect. Given the depth of invasion and involvement of the nearby lymph nodes, a modified radical neck dissection was also performed. Pathology reports confirmed clear resection margins and multiple positive lymph nodes.

The patient with a diagnosis of hypopharyngeal carcinoma underwent a total pharyngolaryngectomy due to extensive tumor infiltration across the piriform sinus. The procedure included the creation of a permanent tracheostomy and an esophageal reconstruction using a jejunal free flap. Exploration revealed multiple metastatic lymph nodes within the cervical region, which were resected during a comprehensive neck dissection. Postoperatively, the patient was advised on the need for adjuvant chemoradiotherapy.

The patient had a malignant lesion involving the left buccal mucosa. A wide local excision was performed, and a split-thickness skin graft was used to cover the resultant defect. The tumor had not penetrated the muscular layer, and no lymph node involvement was detected on preoperative imaging. As a precautionary measure, a sentinel lymph node biopsy was conducted, which returned negative for metastatic disease. The surgical site was closed with meticulous attention to aesthetic outcome.

The patient underwent a partial laryngectomy to excise a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the vocal cord, located in the glottic region. The tumor was confined to the right vocal cord with no extension to the supraglottis or subglottis. Intraoperative frozen section analysis confirmed negative margins. No cervical lymphadenopathy was noted on preoperative imaging, and intraoperatively, no suspicious lymph nodes were palpated. The procedure was uncomplicated, and the patient was extubated in the operating room, showing good airway patency and phonation in recovery.

A transoral robotic surgery (TORS) was performed to resect a T3 squamous cell carcinoma of the base of the tongue. The tumor had extensive invasion into the surrounding musculature but spared the lingual artery. Bilateral selective neck dissection was also conducted, revealing metastatic involvement in the right level II lymph nodes. The procedure was completed without complications, and the patient was transferred to the ICU for postoperative monitoring.

The patient presented with an early-stage carcinoma of the left tonsil. A left tonsillectomy was performed with clear margins achieved. No evidence of local invasion or nodal involvement was found intraoperatively. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was advised to follow up with radiation oncology for adjuvant therapy consideration.

The patient underwent a composite resection involving the floor of the mouth and anterior mandibulectomy for a T4a squamous cell carcinoma of the oral cavity. The tumor showed bony invasion of the mandible, necessitating reconstruction with a fibular free flap. Bilateral neck dissection was performed, revealing metastases in several ipsilateral level I and II lymph nodes. The procedure was complex due to the extensive local spread and required prolonged operative time.

A hypopharyngectomy with total laryngectomy was performed to treat an advanced T4a carcinoma of the hypopharynx. The tumor had invaded the thyroid cartilage and extended into the esophageal inlet. A concurrent bilateral neck dissection was carried out, with metastatic disease found in the left level III lymph nodes. The patient was successfully reconstructed with a gastric pull-up procedure, and a tracheostomy was placed for postoperative airway management.

The patient presented with a moderately advanced squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx, primarily involving the left true vocal cord. The tumor was classified as T3 due to vocal cord fixation. A total laryngectomy was performed, with the removal of the entire larynx and surrounding tissues. Intraoperatively, the tumor was found to extend slightly into the supraglottic region, but there was no gross involvement of the surrounding structures. A neck dissection was conducted, revealing metastatic involvement of the ipsilateral level II and III lymph nodes. The surgical procedure concluded with the patient being fitted with a tracheostomy tube for postoperative airway management.

The patient was diagnosed with a small, localized carcinoma of the tongue, located on the anterior two-thirds of the right side. A partial glossectomy was performed to excise the tumor with clear margins. Intraoperative frozen section analysis confirmed the absence of malignant cells at the resection margins. No lymph node involvement was detected, and the procedure was completed without complications. The patient was closely monitored for any potential postoperative complications, including infection and bleeding.

This patient underwent surgical management for an extensive carcinoma of the hypopharynx, which had invaded the right pyriform sinus. The tumor was staged as T4a due to the invasion of the thyroid cartilage. A pharyngolaryngectomy was performed to remove the affected tissues, with reconstruction using a free jejunal graft to restore continuity of the pharynx. The procedure also included a bilateral neck dissection, which revealed metastases to the right level IV lymph nodes. Postoperative care included intensive monitoring in the ICU due to the complexity of the reconstruction.

The patient, with a history of smoking, presented with a carcinoma of the oropharynx, specifically involving the left tonsillar fossa. A transoral robotic-assisted resection was executed, allowing precise removal of the tumor with minimal disruption to surrounding structures. The tumor was found to be T2, with no evidence of deep tissue invasion. Neck dissection was not performed as preoperative imaging and sentinel node biopsy indicated no lymphatic spread. The patient was discharged on the third postoperative day with instructions for follow-up and adjuvant therapy discussions.

This case involves a carcinoma of the floor of the mouth, extending to the alveolar ridge. The tumor was resected through a marginal mandibulectomy to achieve clear margins while preserving mandibular function. The surgical approach included flap reconstruction using a radial forearm free flap to provide adequate tissue coverage. Metastatic spread was confirmed in the submandibular lymph nodes, necessitating a selective neck dissection. The surgical team ensured meticulous hemostasis and the patient was extubated successfully postoperatively.

The patient presented with a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the right larynx, causing significant hoarseness and mild dysphagia. A partial laryngectomy was performed, successfully excising the tumor with clear margins. Intraoperatively, the tumor was noted to have invaded the superficial lamina propria without further extension. The adjacent arytenoid cartilage was preserved, and a neck dissection was deemed unnecessary as no lymphadenopathy was evident on preoperative imaging.

This 62-year-old male was diagnosed with a T3 carcinoma of the oropharynx, specifically involving the base of the tongue and extending to the ipsilateral tonsillar region. A transoral robotic surgery (TORS) was performed for tumor resection, followed by a selective neck dissection to address suspected lymph node involvement. Metastatic involvement was confirmed in two of the removed lymph nodes. The surgical site was reconstructed using a radial forearm free flap to restore functional integrity.

The patient with a known history of heavy smoking presented with a T1N0 squamous cell carcinoma of the left buccal mucosa. A wide local excision was conducted, and the specimen margins were clear of tumor cells. No lymph node involvement was detected on clinical examination or imaging, thus no additional surgical intervention was required. Postoperative recovery was uneventful with good preservation of oral function.

A 55-year-old female presented with a large, ulcerated mass in the hypopharynx. Biopsy confirmed a poorly differentiated squamous cell carcinoma. Due to the advanced nature (T4a) of the tumor, a total laryngopharyngectomy was performed, accompanied by bilateral neck dissection. Multiple metastatic nodes were identified on the right side, while the left side showed no evidence of metastasis. The postoperative plan included adjuvant radiotherapy due to the extensive disease burden.

The patient was diagnosed with an early-stage carcinoma of the maxillary sinus. A combined approach involving endoscopic sinus surgery and a Caldwell-Luc procedure was employed to achieve complete tumor resection. The tumor was confined to the sinus cavity with no evidence of invasion into adjacent structures or lymph node metastasis. The surgical margins were clear, and postoperative imaging confirmed complete resection.

The patient underwent a partial glossectomy with selective neck dissection for a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the lateral border of the tongue. The tumor measured 2.5 cm in greatest dimension and extended into the underlying musculature. Margins were clear upon frozen section analysis. The selective neck dissection encompassed levels I-III, with one positive lymph node identified in level II. No extracapsular spread was noted. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was transferred to the recovery room in stable condition.

The surgical team performed a total laryngectomy with bilateral neck dissection for an advanced stage laryngeal carcinoma. The tumor involved the right true vocal cord and extended into the supraglottic and subglottic regions, with fixation of the hemilarynx. Bilateral neck dissections were carried out, and metastatic carcinoma was identified in three lymph nodes on the right side and two on the left. Post-operatively, a tracheostomy was placed, and the patient was extubated without complications.

The patient presented with a recurrent carcinoma of the left tonsil. A transoral robotic-assisted resection of the tumor was performed, followed by reconstruction using a radial forearm free flap. The carcinoma was invasive, extending into the musculature of the oropharynx. Clear margins were achieved, and no lymphatic invasion was observed on pathological examination. The patient was stable post-surgery and transferred to the intensive care unit for monitoring.

A wide local excision of the left buccal mucosa was carried out for a superficial squamous cell carcinoma. The lesion was 1.2 cm in size, non-invasive, and situated in the mid-buccal mucosa, with clear peripheral and deep margins confirmed histologically. No neck dissection was deemed necessary due to the lack of clinical and radiological evidence of nodal involvement. The procedure was uncomplicated, and the patient recovered well.

The patient underwent a complex resection of the hypopharynx for a T3 carcinoma with adjacent tissue and muscle invasion. The surgery included a partial pharyngectomy and a left-sided modified radical neck dissection, given the presence of clinically positive nodes. Pathology confirmed metastatic involvement of two level III lymph nodes, with one exhibiting extracapsular extension. The patient required a nasogastric tube for feeding post-operatively and was monitored in the surgical intensive care unit.

The patient, a 58-year-old male, presented with a T2 lesion on the right vocal cord, causing progressive hoarseness. A direct laryngoscopy with biopsy confirmed a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. The patient underwent a partial laryngectomy with preservation of swallowing and voice functions. Surgical margins were clear of tumor, and no lymph node involvement was detected during the procedure.

A 65-year-old female with a long history of tobacco use presented with a painful ulcerative lesion on the right lateral tongue. Biopsy revealed invasive squamous cell carcinoma. The patient underwent a right partial glossectomy with ipsilateral neck dissection, which revealed metastatic involvement of level II and III lymph nodes. Post-operative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was referred for adjuvant radiotherapy.

A 70-year-old male presented with dysphagia and weight loss. Examination revealed a mass in the right oropharynx extending to the tonsillar region. Biopsy confirmed poorly differentiated carcinoma. The patient underwent a transoral robotic-assisted resection of the oropharyngeal tumor with bilateral modified radical neck dissection. Pathology reported positive margins, and multiple lymph nodes were positive for metastases in the bilateral neck.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, presented with a painless neck mass. Imaging and fine needle aspiration confirmed a primary tumor in the hypopharynx with metastatic spread to the cervical lymph nodes. The patient underwent a total laryngopharyngectomy with bilateral neck dissection. Reconstruction was performed using a free jejunal flap. Post-operative course was complicated by a minor wound infection but resolved with antibiotics.

A 55-year-old female presented with a non-healing ulcer on the floor of the mouth. Incisional biopsy confirmed squamous cell carcinoma. The patient underwent wide local excision of the lesion with excision of adjacent sublingual glands. No lymph node dissection was performed as imaging studies showed no nodal involvement. Margins were negative, and the patient will be monitored closely in follow-up visits.

The patient underwent a partial glossectomy for a well-differentiated squamous cell carcinoma located on the lateral border of the tongue. The tumor measured approximately 2.5 cm in its greatest dimension and was excised with clear margins. Intraoperative frozen section analysis confirmed the absence of carcinoma at the margins. Additionally, a selective neck dissection was performed, revealing two out of fifteen lymph nodes positive for metastatic disease. The patient tolerated the procedure well with no immediate postoperative complications.

A total laryngectomy was performed for an advanced carcinoma of the larynx involving the vocal cords bilaterally. The tumor extended to the anterior commissure and into the subglottic region. A radical neck dissection on the right side was also executed, which revealed metastatic spread to the cervical lymph nodes. The surgical team achieved complete resection of the tumor with negative margins. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was transferred to the ICU for monitoring.

The patient presented with a T1 squamous cell carcinoma of the right tonsil. A transoral robotic-assisted surgery was performed for the resection of the tumor. The procedure included a wide local excision, and the margins were confirmed to be free of disease intraoperatively. No neck dissection was deemed necessary as preoperative imaging showed no evidence of lymphadenopathy. The patient was discharged on postoperative day two and scheduled for outpatient follow-up.

A hemimandibulectomy was carried out for a poorly differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the floor of the mouth. The tumor invaded the adjacent mandibular bone, necessitating resection and immediate reconstruction with a fibula free flap. A modified radical neck dissection was performed on the left side, and three out of twenty-two lymph nodes were positive for metastases. The surgery was completed without complications, and the patient was extubated in the operating room.

The patient underwent surgical excision of a squamous cell carcinoma located in the right pyriform sinus. The carcinoma was staged as T2N0M0, and the resection involved a partial pharyngectomy with preservation of the laryngeal function. All resection margins were clear of tumor involvement, and no lymph node dissection was performed due to the absence of nodal disease on imaging. The patient had an uneventful recovery and was discharged with a liquid diet.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, presented with a T2 lesion on the right vocal cord. A direct laryngoscopy was performed, revealing a 2 cm exophytic mass confined to the right true vocal cord. A CO2 laser excision was carried out to remove the lesion, and the margins were confirmed to be clear using frozen section analysis. No lymph nodes were palpable, and the surrounding structures appeared normal. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was extubated in the operating room without complications.

A 55-year-old female was diagnosed with a poorly differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the left tonsil. Examination revealed a 3 cm ulcerative lesion with ipsilateral palpable cervical lymphadenopathy. A transoral robotic-assisted resection of the oropharyngeal tumor was performed, followed by a left modified radical neck dissection. Intraoperative frozen section confirmed metastatic involvement of level II and III lymph nodes. The procedure was completed without incident, and the patient was transferred to recovery in stable condition.

The patient, a 70-year-old male, presented with dysphagia and was found to have a 4 cm mass in the right piriform sinus on CT imaging. A partial pharyngectomy was performed, with reconstruction using a radial forearm free flap. The tumor was excised with adequate margins, and bilateral selective neck dissection was conducted, revealing metastatic spread to bilateral level II lymph nodes. The patient was stabilized postoperatively and transferred to the ICU for monitoring.

A 46-year-old female with a history of smoking presented with a 2 cm lesion on the floor of the mouth. An intraoral wide local excision was performed, with the resection extending to the mandible. Margins were confirmed clear of dysplasia. There was no clinical or radiological evidence of nodal involvement. The surgery concluded uneventfully, and the patient was advised on postoperative oral care and monitoring.

The patient, a 58-year-old male, had a biopsy-confirmed carcinoma of the nasopharynx. An endoscopic nasopharyngectomy was performed to excise the 3.5 cm tumor. Imaging had shown bilateral lymphadenopathy in the cervical region, and a bilateral neck dissection was performed. Metastatic carcinoma was found in levels II, III, and IV nodes on the right side. The patient was extubated post-surgery and transferred to the ward in stable condition.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, presented with a persistent sore throat and difficulty swallowing. Upon examination, a mass was identified in the right tonsillar region. A biopsy confirmed squamous cell carcinoma of the right tonsil. The patient underwent a transoral robotic surgery for resection of the oropharyngeal tumor. Intraoperatively, the tumor was found to be locally advanced, extending to the soft palate. Margins were clear on pathological examination. A selective neck dissection was performed, revealing metastatic involvement in two ipsilateral cervical lymph nodes.

A 55-year-old female with a history of smoking presented with hoarseness and throat pain. Laryngoscopy revealed a suspicious lesion on the left true vocal cord. A biopsy confirmed the presence of squamous cell carcinoma. The patient underwent a partial laryngectomy with preservation of the airway. Intraoperative findings showed that the tumor was confined to the vocal cord without extension to adjacent structures. No lymph nodes were involved upon histopathological analysis.

A 48-year-old male presented with a non-healing ulcer on the floor of the mouth, with associated numbness of the tongue. A biopsy identified invasive squamous cell carcinoma. The patient underwent a wide local excision of the tumor with partial glossectomy. Margins were clear but close. A concurrent bilateral neck dissection was performed, and metastatic spread was confirmed in the submandibular lymph nodes.

The patient, a 70-year-old male, was referred for evaluation of a neck mass and dysphagia. Imaging and subsequent biopsy revealed an advanced squamous cell carcinoma originating in the hypopharynx with extensive lymph node involvement. The patient underwent a total laryngopharyngectomy, including bilateral neck dissection. The tumor was noted to extend into the esophageal inlet, and multiple lymph nodes showed metastatic disease.

A 60-year-old female presented with difficulty swallowing and weight loss. Endoscopic evaluation revealed a mass at the base of the tongue. Biopsy confirmed squamous cell carcinoma. The patient underwent a transoral resection of the tumor. Intraoperatively, the tumor involved the lingual tonsil but did not extend to the epiglottis. A neck dissection was performed with no evidence of lymph node metastasis.

The patient, a 65-year-old male, underwent a total laryngectomy with bilateral neck dissection due to an advanced squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. The tumor was primarily located in the glottic region, with extension into the right piriform sinus. Extensive lymph node involvement was noted on the right side, necessitating a comprehensive removal of affected nodes. The surgical procedure was completed without complications, and the patient was successfully extubated post-operatively. Pathological examination confirmed poorly differentiated carcinoma with extracapsular spread in several right cervical lymph nodes.

A 54-year-old female presented with a rapidly enlarging mass in the right tonsillar region. Biopsy confirmed squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx. The patient underwent transoral robotic surgery (TORS) for resection of the oropharyngeal tumor. The procedure included a right-sided selective neck dissection due to clinically suspicious lymphadenopathy. Final pathology showed a moderately differentiated carcinoma confined to the oropharynx, with no lymphovascular invasion and negative margins.

A 70-year-old male with a history of tobacco use was diagnosed with a carcinoma of the floor of the mouth. He underwent a composite resection of the primary lesion, which involved partial mandibulectomy and reconstruction with a fibular free flap. The tumor was localized with no evidence of regional metastasis. Intraoperative frozen section analysis confirmed clear margins, and the patient was transferred to the ICU for postoperative monitoring.

The patient, a 48-year-old male, presented with dysphagia and was found to have a T3 hypopharyngeal carcinoma. A partial pharyngectomy with reconstruction using a radial forearm free flap was performed. A left neck dissection was also necessary due to involvement of level II and III lymph nodes. Post-surgery, the patient was stable, with plans for adjuvant chemoradiation. Pathology revealed poorly differentiated squamous cell carcinoma with positive lymph nodes.

A 60-year-old female with a persistent ulcer on her lower lip was diagnosed with squamous cell carcinoma of the lip. A wide local excision was performed with primary closure. Sentinel lymph node biopsy was negative, indicating no metastatic spread. The patient tolerated the procedure well, with minimal postoperative pain and no evidence of recurrence on follow-up.

The patient, a 59-year-old male with a history of heavy tobacco use, presented with a persistent hoarse voice and difficulty swallowing. Upon examination, a 3 cm exophytic mass was identified on the right true vocal cord. Direct laryngoscopy and biopsy confirmed squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. The patient underwent a partial laryngectomy with preservation of speech function. Intraoperatively, the tumor was noted to invade the superficial lamina propria without further deep invasion. No lymphadenopathy was observed, and the surgical margins were clear.

A 67-year-old female with a non-healing ulcer on the left side of the tongue was diagnosed with squamous cell carcinoma of the oral cavity. Imaging studies revealed a 2.5 cm lesion with involvement of the floor of the mouth. The patient underwent a left hemiglossectomy and resection of the involved floor of mouth. Bilateral neck dissection was performed, which revealed metastasis to two ipsilateral cervical lymph nodes. Reconstruction was achieved with a radial forearm free flap.

The patient is a 72-year-old male with a history of alcohol abuse presenting with odynophagia and a noticeable mass in the neck. Fiberoptic examination revealed a large ulcerative lesion in the vallecula. A biopsy confirmed poorly differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the hypopharynx. The patient underwent a total pharyngolaryngectomy with bilateral neck dissection. Intraoperative findings showed extensive invasion into the prevertebral fascia and bilateral lymph node involvement.

A 50-year-old male presented with a rapidly enlarging mass in the right parotid region, initially suspected as a benign pleomorphic adenoma. Fine needle aspiration suggested high-grade mucoepidermoid carcinoma. A total parotidectomy was performed, with preservation of the facial nerve. The tumor extended to the deep lobe, and perineural invasion was evident. No evidence of regional lymph node metastasis was found.

A 65-year-old woman with a 3-month history of nasal obstruction and epistaxis was found to have a mass in the nasopharynx. Biopsy confirmed nasopharyngeal carcinoma. The patient underwent endoscopic nasopharyngectomy. During surgery, the tumor was noted to encroach on the sphenoid sinus, and clear surgical margins were achieved. There were no signs of cervical lymphadenopathy.

The patient, a 59-year-old male, presented with a progressively enlarging mass in the right neck and hoarseness. Examination revealed a 3 cm exophytic tumor on the right true vocal cord, extending to the anterior commissure. A right hemilaryngectomy was performed with preservation of the contralateral vocal cord. Intraoperatively, the tumor was noted to invade the thyroid cartilage. A selective neck dissection was also conducted due to palpable lymphadenopathy. Pathological examination confirmed squamous cell carcinoma with metastasis to two of the dissected lymph nodes.

A 47-year-old female with a history of smoking presented with a non-healing ulcer on the left lateral tongue. Biopsy confirmed squamous cell carcinoma. The patient underwent a partial glossectomy with reconstruction using a radial forearm free flap. Margins were clear, but the tumor was found to invade the intrinsic muscles of the tongue. No neck dissection was performed as preoperative imaging showed no nodal involvement.

The patient is a 63-year-old male with a history of heavy alcohol use, presenting with dysphagia and weight loss. Imaging revealed a mass in the hypopharynx involving the pyriform sinus. A total laryngopharyngectomy was performed with a jejunal free flap for reconstruction. The tumor was staged as T4 due to invasion of the surrounding structures. Bilateral neck dissections were performed, and metastatic disease was found in three lymph nodes on the left side.

A 54-year-old female presented with a sore throat and difficulty swallowing. Examination and imaging revealed a 2 cm lesion on the right tonsil, extending to the soft palate. A transoral robotic resection was performed, and the surgical margins were negative. A simultaneous neck dissection was conducted due to suspicious lymph nodes on the imaging, but no metastatic disease was found in the nodes.

The patient, a 70-year-old male, presented with a long-standing ulcerative lesion on the right buccal mucosa. Biopsy confirmed squamous cell carcinoma. The patient underwent wide local excision of the lesion with clear margins. Given the tumor's size and location, a decision was made to perform a prophylactic neck dissection, which showed no evidence of metastatic spread.

The patient underwent a partial glossectomy for a squamous cell carcinoma located on the lateral border of the tongue. The tumor measured 2.5 cm in greatest dimension, and the margins were clear of invasive carcinoma. There was no evidence of perineural invasion. A selective neck dissection was performed, removing levels I-III lymph nodes. Two out of fifteen nodes contained metastatic squamous cell carcinoma, with extracapsular spread noted.

The patient presented with a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the supraglottic larynx. A laryngectomy was performed, including the removal of the epiglottis, false vocal cords, and part of the thyroid cartilage. The surgical margins were clear, and there was no invasion of the prevertebral fascia or involvement of the esophagus. A radical neck dissection was also completed, with no metastatic involvement found in the 20 lymph nodes excised.

The patient was diagnosed with a T4a squamous cell carcinoma of the right maxillary sinus. The surgery involved a total maxillectomy and resection of the tumor invading the anterior wall and floor of the sinus. The resection included part of the hard palate and the inferior orbital rim, with negative margins achieved. No lymph node dissection was performed as there was no clinical or radiological evidence of cervical metastasis.

A 52-year-old female underwent a composite resection for a carcinoma of the left retromolar trigone, involving the soft palate and adjacent buccal mucosa. The tumor was classified as T3 with invasion into the adjacent muscle. The resection included a segment of the mandible due to periosteal invasion. A bilateral neck dissection was performed, and metastases were found in four out of 22 lymph nodes, with one node showing extracapsular extension.

The patient was treated for a recurrent squamous cell carcinoma of the hypopharynx with a history of prior radiation therapy. A total laryngopharyngectomy was performed, including resection of the posterior pharyngeal wall and upper esophagus. Reconstruction was achieved with a free jejunal flap. The tumor was found to infiltrate the prevertebral fascia, but surgical margins were clear. No nodal metastases were identified in the 18 lymph nodes removed during a bilateral neck dissection.

The patient, a 58-year-old male, underwent a partial laryngectomy for a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the glottis. The tumor was localized to the left vocal cord, with no extension to the supraglottic or subglottic regions. Margins were confirmed negative by frozen section analysis intraoperatively. There was no involvement of the anterior commissure. No lymphadenopathy was noted in the neck, and the procedure was completed without complications. The patient will follow up in the head and neck oncology clinic for adjuvant therapy consideration.

A 64-year-old female presented with a locally advanced carcinoma of the oropharynx, primarily involving the left tonsillar fossa. The patient underwent a radical tonsillectomy with ipsilateral neck dissection. The tumor invaded the surrounding soft tissue but was contained within the tonsillar capsule. Multiple lymph nodes were excised, with metastatic involvement confirmed in two out of fifteen nodes. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient is scheduled for adjuvant chemoradiation.

The procedure involved a wide local excision of a T1 lesion in the floor of the mouth of a 45-year-old male. The lesion spanned 1.5 cm and was confined to the mucosa, with no evidence of bone invasion. Clear margins were achieved, and there was no clinical or radiological evidence of regional lymph node involvement. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was discharged with instructions for regular follow-up.

A 70-year-old male underwent a total parotidectomy for a malignant tumor of the deep lobe of the parotid gland. The tumor extended into the stylomastoid foramen, requiring a resection of the adjacent facial nerve. Intraoperative nerve monitoring was utilized, and a nerve graft was performed to restore function. Two lymph nodes were positive for metastatic disease in the upper cervical chain, necessitating postoperative radiation therapy.

The patient, a 55-year-old female, underwent a hemiglossectomy for a T3 squamous cell carcinoma of the lateral border of the tongue. The cancer extended into the base but spared the midline. Reconstruction was performed using a radial forearm free flap. There was no evidence of lymph node metastasis on preoperative imaging, and the neck dissection revealed no pathological nodes. The patient is planned for close surveillance.

The patient underwent a partial glossectomy for a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the left lateral border of the tongue. The tumor measured 2.5 cm in greatest dimension and was found to invade the underlying muscle tissue. Margins were clear with a 1 cm clearance. A selective neck dissection was performed, involving levels I-III, due to suspected lymph node involvement. Two lymph nodes were positive for metastatic carcinoma. The procedure was completed without complications, and the patient was extubated and transferred to the recovery unit in stable condition.

Surgical excision of a T1N0M0 squamous cell carcinoma of the right vocal cord was performed using microlaryngoscopy. The lesion, which measured 1.0 cm, was confined to the superficial layer of the lamina propria. No extralaryngeal spread was noted, and the contralateral vocal cord appeared normal. The operation was conducted under general anesthesia, with careful preservation of the surrounding structures. The patient was discharged with instructions for voice rest and follow-up in the outpatient clinic in two weeks.

The patient presented with a locally advanced squamous cell carcinoma of the right tonsil, staged as T3N2bM0. A transoral robotic-assisted radical tonsillectomy was performed, combined with a modified radical neck dissection on the right side. The tumor extended into the surrounding soft palate but did not infiltrate the base of the tongue. A total of 28 lymph nodes were removed, with metastasis confirmed in five nodes. Postoperative recovery was unremarkable, and the patient was referred for adjuvant chemoradiotherapy.

A maxillectomy was performed for a poorly differentiated carcinoma of the left hard palate. The tumor was eroding into the maxillary sinus and measured 4.5 cm at its widest point. Reconstruction was achieved using a radial forearm free flap, and intraoperative frozen section analysis confirmed clear margins. No lymphadenopathy was observed, and the patient tolerated the procedure well. The patient was transferred to the intensive care unit for postoperative monitoring.

The patient underwent a total laryngectomy with bilateral neck dissection for an advanced laryngeal squamous cell carcinoma, classified as T4aN2cM0. The tumor was invading through the thyroid cartilage and involved both vocal cords. Lymph node involvement was extensive, with positive nodes on both sides of the neck. The trachea was brought out as a stoma. The patient was placed on a feeding tube postoperatively and was stable in the recovery room, with plans for rehabilitation and speech therapy.

The patient underwent a partial glossectomy to excise a well-defined squamous cell carcinoma located on the lateral border of the tongue. Intraoperative findings confirmed the tumor was approximately 2 cm in size, with no evident infiltration into the deep musculature. Margins were assessed intraoperatively, ensuring a clear boundary from the cancerous tissue. A selective neck dissection was also performed, targeting levels I-III, due to suspicion of regional metastasis. No significant nodal involvement was observed upon initial frozen section analysis.

A total laryngectomy was performed on a 65-year-old male patient who presented with a T3 tumor of the supraglottic larynx. The tumor had extended into the pre-epiglottic space but had not yet breached the thyroid cartilage. A tracheostomy was established post-resection. Bilateral neck dissection was conducted, and multiple enlarged lymph nodes were excised from the left side, which were later confirmed to be metastatic. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was planned for adjuvant radiotherapy.

A radical resection of the base of the tongue was necessitated by a 4 cm invasive squamous cell carcinoma extending into the lingual tonsil region. The procedure included a bilateral tonsillectomy and removal of the involved soft palate tissue. The tumor was noted to have perineural invasion but no vascular involvement. A right-sided modified radical neck dissection was performed due to palpable lymphadenopathy. Histopathological examination confirmed the presence of metastatic carcinoma in two of the dissected nodes.

The patient underwent a resection of a T1 squamous cell carcinoma located on the floor of the mouth. The lesion, measuring 1.5 cm, was excised with a primary closure achieved with local tissue advancement flap. Examination revealed no lymphatic spread, and no neck dissection was deemed necessary. The postoperative period was smooth, with no complications, and the patient was scheduled for close follow-up.

A 58-year-old female underwent a composite resection for a carcinoma of the hypopharynx that involved the piriform sinus and extended to the cervical esophagus. The surgical procedure included a partial pharyngectomy and esophagectomy with reconstruction using a free jejunal flap. A bilateral neck dissection was performed, revealing metastatic spread to the level IV lymph nodes. The surgical margins were clear, and the patient was referred for concurrent chemoradiotherapy.

The patient underwent a total laryngectomy due to a diagnosed squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. During the procedure, the entire larynx was successfully removed along with surrounding tissues to ensure clear margins. A tracheostomy was performed to maintain an airway post-surgery. Additionally, an elective neck dissection revealed metastatic involvement in two of the cervical lymph nodes. Post-operatively, the patient was monitored in the ICU for airway management and recovery from anesthesia.

A radical resection of the oropharynx was conducted on the patient, who presented with an extensive tumor in the base of the tongue. The surgical team excised the tumor along with adjacent tissue and performed a selective neck dissection to assess potential metastatic spread. Pathology confirmed the presence of HPV-positive squamous cell carcinoma, with clean surgical margins and no lymph node involvement.

The patient underwent a maxillectomy for a malignant neoplasm located in the hard palate. The surgery involved the removal of part of the maxilla to ensure complete excision of the tumor. Reconstruction of the defect was achieved using a free flap from the patient's forearm. Post-operative recovery was uneventful, and no lymphatic spread was observed.

The patient was treated with a partial glossectomy for squamous cell carcinoma of the anterior two-thirds of the tongue. Intraoperative examination showed no evidence of infiltration beyond the primary site, and the surgical margins were confirmed clear by frozen section analysis. A simultaneous neck dissection was performed, although no metastatic lymph nodes were identified.

A comprehensive surgical intervention was undertaken for a hypopharyngeal carcinoma, which involved extensive resection of the tumor and surrounding tissues. The procedure included a pharyngectomy and selective neck dissection due to suspected metastatic involvement of the cervical lymph nodes. Post-operative care included nutritional support and speech therapy, given the significant impact on the patient's swallowing and vocal functions.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, presented with a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the left vocal cord. The lesion was excised using a CO2 laser under direct laryngoscopy. Intraoperative frozen sections confirmed clear margins. The patient tolerated the procedure well with minimal bleeding. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was extubated without complications. Regular follow-up appointments were scheduled to monitor for recurrence.

A 54-year-old female with a history of smoking presented with a mass in the right tonsil. Imaging studies revealed a T3 tumor with ipsilateral cervical lymphadenopathy. A right-sided tonsillectomy with radical neck dissection was performed. The tumor was confined to the tonsil, and all involved lymph nodes were successfully excised. The surgical team ensured hemostasis and the patient was transferred to recovery in stable condition. Pathology confirmed metastasis to two lymph nodes.

The patient, a 70-year-old male, underwent a partial glossectomy for a T1 carcinoma located on the lateral border of the tongue. The tumor was excised with a 1 cm margin and the defect was closed primarily. No lymphadenopathy was observed. The procedure was uncomplicated and the patient was discharged on postoperative day two with instructions for dietary modifications and speech therapy.

A 45-year-old female presented with dysphagia and was found to have a T4a tumor in the hypopharynx extending to the esophageal inlet. A total laryngopharyngectomy with gastric pull-up was performed. The complex procedure involved reconstructing the alimentary tract and maintaining airway continuity. Several enlarged cervical lymph nodes were resected, and pathology reported metastatic spread. The patient was monitored in the ICU postoperatively and began oral intake after two weeks.

A 58-year-old male presented with a T2 lesion at the base of the tongue with no palpable cervical adenopathy. A transoral robotic-assisted base of tongue resection was executed. Margins were verified intraoperatively, ensuring tumor-free tissue. The procedure was completed without complications, and the patient was extubated in the operating room. Postoperative care included pain management and speech therapy to address mild dysarthria.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, underwent a total laryngectomy due to a T3 squamous cell carcinoma located in the glottic region of the larynx. Intraoperatively, the tumor was found to invade the paraglottic space but without extralaryngeal extension. A right modified radical neck dissection was performed due to palpable cervical lymphadenopathy. Pathological examination confirmed metastatic involvement in two of the 15 dissected nodes at levels II and III. The surgical margins were clear of tumor, and the patient tolerated the procedure well.

A 54-year-old female presented with a T2 carcinoma of the base of the tongue. A transoral robotic surgery (TORS) was performed to excise the tumor, followed by bilateral selective neck dissection. The tumor was confined to the base of the tongue without infiltration into surrounding tissues. Metastatic carcinoma was identified in one of the left-sided level II nodes. The patient had an uneventful recovery.

A 47-year-old male was diagnosed with a large T4a squamous cell carcinoma of the right maxillary sinus. The patient underwent a right total maxillectomy with orbital preservation, and an ipsilateral neck dissection. The tumor extended to the anterior wall of the maxilla and involved the infraorbital nerve. No lymphatic spread was observed in the 18 nodes dissected. Postoperative recovery was complicated by a minor wound infection.

The patient, a 70-year-old male with a history of smoking, presented with a T1 squamous cell carcinoma of the floor of the mouth. A wide local excision was performed with reconstruction using a radial forearm free flap. The tumor was superficial, involving only the mucosa without muscle invasion. No neck dissection was needed as imaging showed no evidence of lymph node involvement. The margins were clear on final pathology.

A 65-year-old female underwent a partial pharyngectomy for a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the hypopharynx. The tumor was located in the piriform sinus and extended into the post-cricoid area. A left selective neck dissection was performed, revealing metastatic involvement in two out of 20 nodes at level IV. The resection margins were negative, and the patient was discharged with a feeding tube for nutritional support during recovery.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, presented with a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the supraglottic larynx. A partial laryngectomy was performed, preserving the arytenoid and contralateral vocal cord. Intraoperative frozen sections confirmed clear margins. The right level II-III neck dissection revealed metastatic involvement in two out of ten lymph nodes. Post-surgical recovery was uneventful, and the patient is scheduled for adjuvant radiotherapy.

A 55-year-old female with a history of tobacco use underwent a wide local excision of a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma located at the base of the tongue. The tumor measured 3.5 cm in greatest dimension and extended into the adjacent soft tissue. A selective neck dissection was performed, revealing no nodal metastasis. The surgical margins were clear, and the patient was advised on cessation programs for tobacco use.

An 80-year-old male presented with an aggressive poorly differentiated carcinoma of the hypopharynx, extending into the post-cricoid region. The surgical team performed a total pharyngolaryngectomy with bilateral neck dissection. Pathological analysis showed extensive perineural invasion and metastasis in four lymph nodes. The patient is being evaluated for potential enrollment in a clinical trial for targeted therapy.

The patient, a 45-year-old male, underwent a maxillectomy for a T1 squamous cell carcinoma of the hard palate. The lesion measured 1.2 cm and was excised with a 1 cm margin, which was confirmed to be clear of tumor upon pathological examination. No neck dissection was performed as imaging showed no evidence of nodal disease. The patient will follow up with dental prosthetics for rehabilitation.

A 69-year-old female presented with a recurrent T4 squamous cell carcinoma of the left tonsil, with prior chemoradiation therapy. A radical tonsillectomy and modified radical neck dissection were performed, revealing extensive extracapsular spread in nodal tissue. Surgical margins were microscopically involved, necessitating further adjuvant therapy. The patient was counseled on the potential benefits of reconstructive surgery post-treatment.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, presented with a 3 cm exophytic mass on the right vocal cord, diagnosed as squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. The tumor was confined to the larynx with no evidence of invasion into surrounding structures. A partial laryngectomy was performed, preserving the contralateral vocal cord. The surgical margins were negative for malignancy. Postoperatively, the patient showed satisfactory recovery with preserved voice function. No lymphadenopathy was noted on preoperative imaging.

A 55-year-old female with a history of smoking presented with a 4 cm ulcerative lesion at the base of the tongue. Biopsy confirmed squamous cell carcinoma. The patient underwent a transoral robotic surgery for resection of the oropharyngeal tumor. Intraoperatively, the tumor was found to invade the lingual tonsil and the posterior pharyngeal wall. A left neck dissection was also performed due to palpable lymphadenopathy, revealing metastatic involvement in two lymph nodes. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was referred for adjuvant radiotherapy.

An 80-year-old male was diagnosed with a 2.5 cm squamous cell carcinoma of the right buccal mucosa. The tumor extended to the retromolar trigone without bony involvement. A wide local excision with 1 cm margins was performed, along with reconstruction using a radial forearm free flap. No lymph node dissection was deemed necessary as imaging showed no evidence of nodal involvement. The patient recovered well post-surgery and was advised regular follow-ups for surveillance.

A 45-year-old male presented with a rapidly enlarging mass in the left supraglottic region, causing significant airway obstruction. A CT scan revealed a 5 cm poorly differentiated carcinoma with involvement of the left aryepiglottic fold and extension to the left arytenoid cartilage. An emergency tracheostomy was performed to secure the airway, followed by a total laryngectomy. Bilateral modified radical neck dissection was carried out, with metastatic deposits found in multiple cervical lymph nodes. The patient was discharged with a speech valve and plans for adjuvant chemoradiation.

The patient, a 70-year-old female, presented with a non-healing ulcer on the hard palate. Biopsy confirmed a diagnosis of squamous cell carcinoma. The tumor measured 1.8 cm and was confined to the hard palate without invasion into the maxillary bone. A maxillectomy was performed, and intraoperative frozen sections confirmed clear margins. No neck dissection was performed as there was no clinical or radiological evidence of regional metastases. The patient was fitted with an obturator and has adapted well post-surgery, with no signs of recurrence at follow-up.

The patient presented with a stage II squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx, involving the glottic region. During the procedure, a partial laryngectomy was performed to excise the tumor, preserving the contralateral vocal cord and arytenoid. The surgical margins were confirmed to be clear through intraoperative frozen sections. No lymph node involvement was noted during the accompanying neck dissection. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient has been scheduled for adjuvant radiotherapy to ensure local control.

This case involved a 62-year-old male with a diagnosed squamous cell carcinoma of the base of the tongue, classified as stage III. The surgical team conducted a transoral robotic surgery (TORS) to resect the primary tumor. In addition, a selective neck dissection was performed, revealing metastatic involvement of the ipsilateral level IIA lymph nodes. The patient tolerated the procedure well, with no intraoperative complications, and was discharged with an appropriate pain management regimen.

The patient, a 57-year-old female, was diagnosed with a T2N0M0 squamous cell carcinoma of the left tonsil. A tonsillectomy with wide local excision was performed, and the tumor was completely removed with negative margins. No lymphadenopathy was observed on preoperative imaging, and intraoperative evaluation confirmed the absence of suspicious nodes. The surgery proceeded without complications, and the patient was referred for consideration of adjuvant therapy based on multidisciplinary team recommendations.

A 50-year-old male with a history of tobacco use presented with a large, ulcerative lesion in the floor of the mouth, confirmed as squamous cell carcinoma. A composite resection was undertaken, including partial mandibulectomy and resection of the involved oral mucosa. Reconstruction was achieved using a free fibular flap. A comprehensive neck dissection revealed metastatic disease in multiple cervical lymph nodes. Postoperative recovery was complicated by wound infection, which was managed with antibiotics and surgical debridement.

The patient was diagnosed with a carcinoma of the hypopharynx extending to the postcricoid area. A total laryngopharyngectomy was performed, followed by the reconstruction of the pharyngeal passage using a radial forearm free flap. The surgical team also conducted a bilateral neck dissection, which found metastatic spread to the right level III lymph nodes. The patient was closely monitored in the ICU postoperatively, with an uneventful recovery, and has been placed on a course of adjuvant chemotherapy.

The patient, a 58-year-old male, underwent a partial laryngectomy for a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the glottic larynx. The tumor was confined to the left true vocal cord, with no extension to the adjacent structures. Surgical margins were clear, and there was no evidence of perineural invasion. A selective neck dissection was performed, removing levels II-IV lymph nodes, with no metastasis found. The patient tolerated the procedure well with no immediate complications.

A 62-year-old female presented with a large ulcerative lesion at the base of the tongue. The patient underwent a transoral robotic surgery (TORS) resection of the oropharyngeal tumor. The pathology confirmed a poorly differentiated squamous cell carcinoma, with invasion into the right tonsillar fossa. Margins were clear, but due to the size and depth of the tumor, a bilateral neck dissection was performed. Metastatic involvement was identified in a level III lymph node on the right side.

The patient, a 45-year-old male, underwent wide local excision of a squamous cell carcinoma located on the left lateral border of the tongue. The tumor was classified as T1 with no evidence of lymphovascular invasion. A sentinel lymph node biopsy was negative for metastatic disease. The postoperative course was uneventful, and the patient was discharged with instructions for follow-up in the oncology clinic.

A 70-year-old male with a history of heavy smoking presented with a mass in the left pyriform sinus. A total laryngopharyngectomy was performed, with reconstruction using a free jejunal flap. The tumor was a T3 squamous cell carcinoma with invasion into the thyroid cartilage. Lymph node metastases were identified in levels II and IV on the left side. The patient was stable postoperatively, with plans for adjuvant radiation therapy.

The patient, a 55-year-old female, underwent resection of a buccal mucosa tumor. The lesion was a T2 poorly differentiated squamous cell carcinoma, extending to the alveolar ridge. Margins were clear, and an ipsilateral neck dissection showed no lymph node involvement. The patient's postoperative recovery was unremarkable, and she is scheduled for routine follow-up to monitor for recurrence.

The patient underwent a partial glossectomy for a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma located at the lateral border of the tongue. The tumor measured approximately 2.5 cm in greatest dimension and was confined to the tongue, with no invasion into surrounding structures. A neck dissection was performed, and two lymph nodes were found to be positive for metastatic disease. Hemostasis was achieved, and the surgical site was closed in layers. The patient was extubated and transferred to the recovery room in stable condition.

A total laryngectomy was performed on a patient with stage IV laryngeal carcinoma. The tumor involved the entire glottic region and extended into the subglottis. The resection included the removal of the thyroid cartilage and adjacent soft tissues. Bilateral neck dissection was carried out, revealing metastatic spread to the right level II lymph nodes. Reconstruction of the pharynx was accomplished using a radial forearm free flap. The procedure was completed without complications, and the patient was monitored closely in the intensive care unit post-operatively.

The patient presented with a T1N0M0 squamous cell carcinoma of the left tonsil. An intraoral approach was used to perform a tonsillectomy with clear margins. The tumor was well-circumscribed and did not show any signs of extracapsular spread. No neck dissection was deemed necessary as there was no clinical or radiological evidence of lymph node involvement. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was discharged on the same day with instructions for follow-up in the outpatient clinic.

A composite resection was performed for an extensive squamous cell carcinoma involving the floor of the mouth and extending to the lower alveolar ridge. The surgery included partial mandibulectomy with segmental resection of the mandible. Intraoperative frozen sections confirmed negative margins. A modified radical neck dissection on the ipsilateral side revealed metastatic involvement in levels I and II lymph nodes. The defect was reconstructed using a fibula free flap. Post-operative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was discharged with appropriate dietary and physical therapy instructions.

The patient underwent transoral robotic surgery (TORS) for a small squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx, specifically the base of the tongue. The lesion was confined to the mucosal surface without deep tissue invasion. No neck dissection was performed as imaging studies showed no nodal involvement. The surgical margins were confirmed to be clear through frozen section analysis. The patient was extubated without difficulty and discharged the following day with a follow-up plan for adjuvant therapy evaluation.

The patient underwent a transoral robotic surgery for a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the base of the tongue. The tumor was located in the right posterior third of the tongue and measured approximately 3.2 cm in its greatest dimension. Margins were clear on final pathology, with 1 cm of healthy tissue surrounding the resected tumor. No evidence of perineural invasion was noted. A selective neck dissection was performed, and four lymph nodes were excised, none showing metastatic involvement on histological examination.

The surgical team performed a wide local excision and partial mandibulectomy for a T4a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the floor of the mouth. The lesion extended to the anterior mandible, necessitating resection of a portion of the bone. The mandibular defect was reconstructed with a fibular free flap. Intraoperative frozen sections confirmed negative margins. Bilateral neck dissections revealed metastatic involvement in two out of thirty nodes on the left side, with extracapsular spread.

The procedure began with a total laryngectomy and bilateral neck dissection for an advanced squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. The tumor had invaded the paraglottic space and extended through the thyroid cartilage. A tracheoesophageal puncture was performed for voice rehabilitation. Pathology confirmed the presence of metastatic carcinoma in eight out of twenty lymph nodes, with significant extracapsular extension noted.

A right-sided maxillectomy was carried out for a patient with recurrent squamous cell carcinoma of the maxillary sinus, originally treated with chemoradiation. The tumor had recurred in the lateral wall of the sinus with involvement of the orbital floor. The resection included the entire maxillary sinus, sparing the orbital contents. Reconstruction was achieved using a radial forearm free flap. Histopathology showed clear resection margins.

The surgical intervention involved a radical resection of a squamous cell carcinoma of the hypopharynx, specifically targeting the piriform sinus. The tumor was classified as T3, having extended into the cervical esophagus. A total esophagectomy was performed with gastric pull-up for reconstruction. No lymphovascular invasion was detected, and only one out of fifteen resected lymph nodes contained metastatic carcinoma.

The patient underwent a partial laryngectomy for a T2 squamous cell carcinoma located at the glottis. The tumor was excised with clear margins, and intraoperative frozen sections confirmed the absence of residual malignant tissue. The right vocal cord was removed, and the anterior commissure was reconstructed. No evidence of spread to the surrounding thyroid cartilage was observed. The surgical field was closed meticulously, and the patient was extubated without complications.

A 58-year-old male presented with a large, ulcerated mass in the right tonsil region, confirmed as squamous cell carcinoma. A transoral robotic surgery (TORS) was performed to excise the primary tumor, followed by a selective neck dissection due to suspected lymph node involvement. Metastatic involvement was found in a single level II lymph node, which was resected. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient showed good oral function and swallowing.

The patient, diagnosed with a T1N0M0 floor of mouth cancer, underwent surgical excision with the aim of achieving negative margins. The procedure involved resection of the lesion along with a small portion of the adjacent gingiva. Reconstruction was performed using a local flap. No lymphadenopathy was noted, and frozen section analysis confirmed clean margins. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was discharged on postoperative day 2.

A complex case of T3 hypopharyngeal carcinoma extending to the esophageal inlet was managed by total laryngopharyngectomy. The tumor showed extensive invasion but was successfully removed with en bloc resection. A radial forearm free flap was utilized for reconstruction of the pharyngeal defect. Bilateral neck dissection was performed, revealing metastatic spread to multiple lymph nodes on the left side. The patient was transferred to the ICU for postoperative management.

The patient underwent a wide local excision for a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the buccal mucosa. The tumor was located on the left side and involved resection of part of the cheek and adjacent mucosal tissue. A split-thickness skin graft was used to close the defect. The procedure was uncomplicated, and the patient was extubated in the operating room. Pathology confirmed clear margins with no lymphovascular invasion.

The patient underwent a partial glossectomy for a T2 tumor located on the lateral border of the tongue, measuring approximately 2.5 cm in diameter. The tumor was excised with clear margins, and a selective neck dissection was performed on the right side, encompassing levels I-III. Intraoperative frozen sections confirmed negative margins. Post-operative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was monitored for potential complications such as bleeding or infection.

A 63-year-old male with a history of smoking and alcohol use presented with a T3 carcinoma of the supraglottic larynx. A total laryngectomy was performed, along with bilateral neck dissection due to suspected metastatic spread. The procedure included a tracheostomy to secure the airway. Pathology confirmed squamous cell carcinoma with metastasis to cervical lymph nodes bilaterally. Post-operative care included speech and swallowing rehabilitation.

The patient, a 54-year-old female, presented with a lesion in the left tonsillar fossa. A tonsillectomy with wide local excision was performed to remove the T1 oropharyngeal tumor. Intraoperative assessment showed no evidence of metastasis, thus no neck dissection was carried out. The patient tolerated the procedure well, with no immediate complications. She is scheduled for postoperative radiotherapy as a precautionary measure.

An 80-year-old male underwent a composite resection for a T4a carcinoma of the floor of the mouth, involving the mandible. The surgery involved partial mandibulectomy with reconstruction using a fibula free flap. Additionally, a bilateral selective neck dissection was performed due to palpable lymphadenopathy. Pathological analysis revealed extensive local invasion but no distant metastasis. The patient is currently in the ICU for post-operative monitoring.

The patient presented with a suspicious mass in the hypopharynx. Biopsy confirmed a T2 squamous cell carcinoma. A partial pharyngectomy was performed, sparing the larynx. The surgical team also conducted a unilateral neck dissection due to enlarged lymph nodes on the left side. Margins were clear, and the lymph nodes were negative for cancer. The patient had an uneventful recovery and was discharged with dietary modifications to aid in swallowing.

The patient underwent a partial glossectomy for resection of a squamous cell carcinoma located on the right lateral border of the tongue. During the procedure, clear margins were achieved, and no extension beyond the muscular layer was noted. An ipsilateral selective neck dissection was performed, yielding three lymph nodes, all of which were negative for metastatic disease. The surgical site was closed primarily, and the patient was extubated without complications.

A 68-year-old male presented with a large, ulcerative lesion in the supraglottic region, diagnosed as squamous cell carcinoma. A total laryngectomy was performed due to the tumor's extensive invasion into the pre-epiglottic space. Bilateral neck dissection was also conducted, with metastatic involvement found in the right level II lymph nodes. The patient was tracheostomized permanently and will require further oncological management post-operatively.

The patient, a 54-year-old female, underwent transoral robotic surgery for a T1 lesion located on the left tonsillar pillar. The tumor was excised with clear margins, and no lymphatic spread was evident on pre-operative imaging. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was discharged with recommendations for regular follow-up and possible adjuvant therapy based on further histological analysis.

A comprehensive surgical approach was taken for a 59-year-old patient with a carcinoma of the right floor of the mouth. The surgical plan included a composite resection with marginal mandibulectomy and radial forearm free flap reconstruction. Additionally, a modified radical neck dissection on the right side revealed metastatic deposits in two out of fifteen dissected lymph nodes. The patient is scheduled for postoperative radiotherapy to address the nodal involvement.

The patient underwent a total thyroidectomy with central neck dissection following the diagnosis of an anaplastic thyroid carcinoma with invasion into the adjacent trachea. Intraoperatively, the tumor was found to be infiltrating the tracheal wall, necessitating partial tracheal resection and reconstruction. Multiple enlarged lymph nodes were also excised, with pathology confirming metastatic spread to the central compartment.

The patient, a 58-year-old male, presented with a persistent sore throat and difficulty swallowing. Upon examination, a lesion was identified on the base of the tongue. A decision was made to perform a partial glossectomy to excise the tumor. Intraoperative findings revealed a 3 cm ulcerative mass at the base of the tongue, with no evidence of invasion into the surrounding structures. A selective neck dissection was performed, and one lymph node was found to be enlarged and was excised for pathology. The surgical margins were clear, and the lymph node biopsy was negative for metastases.

A 62-year-old female with a history of smoking presented with hoarseness and a neck mass. Imaging revealed a large tumor involving the left vocal cord and multiple enlarged cervical lymph nodes. The patient underwent a total laryngectomy with bilateral neck dissection. Intraoperatively, the tumor was found to be infiltrating the vocal cord with extension to the paraglottic space. Multiple lymph nodes were dissected, and histopathology confirmed metastatic involvement in three nodes. The procedure was completed without complications, and the patient was referred for adjuvant radiation therapy.

This 70-year-old male came in with a non-healing ulcer on the floor of the mouth. Biopsy had confirmed squamous cell carcinoma. A wide local excision of the floor of the mouth was performed, including a marginal mandibulectomy to ensure clear margins. The tumor measured 2.5 cm in greatest dimension and was confined to the mucosa without evidence of bony invasion. No lymphadenopathy was noted. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was discharged with instructions for close follow-up.

A 45-year-old female presented with dysphagia and a mass in the right oropharynx. Examination revealed a 4 cm exophytic mass involving the tonsillar fossa extending to the soft palate. A transoral robotic-assisted resection of the oropharyngeal tumor was performed. Additionally, a right neck dissection was conducted, which revealed metastatic carcinoma in two of the dissected nodes. The surgical margins were negative, and the patient was scheduled for postoperative chemoradiation therapy due to lymph node involvement.

The patient, a 55-year-old male, was referred for evaluation of a large hypopharyngeal tumor causing significant dysphonia and airway obstruction. A direct laryngoscopy showed an obstructive mass at the pyriform sinus. A total pharyngolaryngectomy with reconstruction using a radial forearm free flap was undertaken. The tumor was noted to invade the cricoid cartilage but did not extend into the surrounding soft tissue. No lymphadenopathy was found intraoperatively, and the histological examination confirmed the absence of nodal metastases.

The patient, a 58-year-old male, presented with a progressively enlarging mass on the right lateral aspect of the tongue. A biopsy confirmed squamous cell carcinoma of the oral tongue. Surgical intervention included a partial glossectomy with clear margins obtained. Intraoperative assessment revealed no palpable lymphadenopathy of the neck. The procedure was completed successfully without complications. The patient is scheduled for close follow-up to monitor for recurrence.

A 67-year-old female with a history of heavy smoking presented with hoarseness and difficulty swallowing. Imaging and biopsy confirmed a malignant neoplasm of the larynx. The patient underwent a total laryngectomy with bilateral neck dissection due to presence of suspicious lymph nodes. Metastatic spread to cervical lymph nodes was confirmed intraoperatively. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient is being considered for adjuvant radiotherapy.

A 72-year-old male with a history of alcohol use presented with a sore throat and a visible mass in the oropharynx. Examination and biopsy established the diagnosis of squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx. The surgical team performed a transoral robotic resection of the oropharyngeal tumor, achieving negative margins. No lymph node involvement was observed. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was discharged with recommendations for speech and swallowing rehabilitation.

The patient, a 62-year-old female, complained of persistent ear pain and a lump in the neck. Evaluation revealed a primary tumor in the hypopharynx with metastatic involvement of regional lymph nodes. The patient underwent a partial pharyngectomy with selective neck dissection. The surgery was complex, given the tumor's proximity to critical structures, but was completed without residual tumor. Postoperative care includes preparation for chemoradiation therapy.

A 55-year-old male presented with a non-healing ulcer on the floor of the mouth. Biopsy confirmed squamous cell carcinoma. The patient underwent surgical resection of the tumor with clear margins, followed by a supraomohyoid neck dissection. Intraoperative findings showed no lymph node metastasis. The patient was advised on oral hygiene and nutritional support to aid recovery and prevent complications.

The patient, a 63-year-old male, presented with a progressively enlarging mass in the left side of the neck, along with hoarseness and difficulty swallowing. A laryngoscopy revealed a fungating mass on the left vocal cord. A biopsy confirmed squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. The patient underwent a total laryngectomy with bilateral neck dissection. The surgery was successful, and the tumor was completely excised with clear margins. Multiple lymph nodes were found to be involved, confirming metastatic spread.

A 56-year-old female with a history of smoking presented with a non-healing ulcer on the right lateral border of the tongue. Biopsy confirmed squamous cell carcinoma of the tongue. The patient underwent a partial glossectomy with selective neck dissection. The tumor was confined to the tongue with no evidence of regional lymph node involvement. The surgery achieved clear margins, and the postoperative recovery was uneventful.

The patient, a 70-year-old male, experienced persistent sore throat and ear pain. Examination and imaging revealed a large mass in the left tonsillar region extending to the soft palate. A biopsy indicated squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx. The patient underwent a transoral robotic surgery (TORS) for tumor resection and a left neck dissection. Pathology confirmed invasion of the tumor into the soft palate and metastatic involvement of cervical lymph nodes.

A 45-year-old female presented with a swelling in the right neck and difficulty breathing. An MRI showed a large mass originating from the hypopharynx with airway compromise. A biopsy confirmed carcinoma. The surgical team performed a partial pharyngectomy with reconstruction and right neck dissection. The mass was excised with clear margins, and metastatic disease was identified in two lymph nodes.

The patient, a 60-year-old male, reported a persistent sore and white patch on the inner cheek. A biopsy was performed, indicating carcinoma of the buccal mucosa. He underwent a wide local excision of the lesion with sentinel lymph node biopsy. The margins were clear, and there was no lymphatic involvement. The patient recovered well postoperatively and was discharged with follow-up care instructions.

The patient underwent a partial glossectomy following the diagnosis of a T2 squamous cell carcinoma located on the lateral border of the tongue. The surgical procedure involved the excision of the tumor with a 1 cm margin of healthy tissue to ensure clear margins. Intraoperative frozen section analysis confirmed negative margins. The submandibular duct was identified and preserved. No evidence of nerve invasion was observed. The patient was stable throughout the procedure and transferred to recovery in satisfactory condition.

A total laryngectomy was performed on a patient with a history of heavy tobacco use, presenting with a T4a carcinoma of the larynx extending to the thyroid cartilage. The procedure involved the removal of the larynx, including the vocal cords, with care taken to preserve the esophagus. Bilateral neck dissection was conducted due to palpable lymphadenopathy, and multiple enlarged lymph nodes were excised for pathological examination. Postoperative pathology confirmed metastatic involvement in level II cervical nodes.

The patient presented with a T1 squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx, specifically involving the left tonsillar pillar. A transoral robotic surgery (TORS) was performed to excise the tumor with adequate margins. The procedure was uneventful, and no lymphatic spread was detected. Postoperative recovery was smooth, and the patient did not exhibit any significant complications.

A patient with a known diagnosis of T3 hypopharyngeal carcinoma underwent a radical resection involving partial pharyngectomy and esophageal reconstruction with a gastric pull-up. Intraoperative findings revealed tumor invasion into the pyriform sinus and prevertebral fascia. A unilateral neck dissection was performed, revealing metastatic involvement of level III and IV lymph nodes. The patient was monitored closely postoperatively in the ICU.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, presented with a recurrent basal cell carcinoma of the nasal cavity. A wide local excision was performed, including the resection of the anterior nasal septum and adjacent soft tissue. Reconstruction was achieved using a local flap technique. The procedure was completed without complication, and pathology confirmed clear surgical margins. No lymph node dissection was performed as there was no clinical evidence of nodal metastasis.

The patient underwent a partial glossectomy for a well-differentiated squamous cell carcinoma located on the right lateral border of the tongue. The tumor measured 2.5 cm in greatest dimension. Margins were taken circumferentially, and frozen sections were negative for malignancy. A selective neck dissection was performed, targeting levels I-III to address palpable lymphadenopathy. Three lymph nodes were found to be positive for metastatic disease, confirming cervical nodal involvement. Hemostasis was achieved, and the surgical site was closed in layers. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was transferred to the recovery room in stable condition.

The patient presented with a T1N0M0 squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx, specifically involving the left vocal cord. A transoral laser microsurgery was conducted to excise the tumor. The affected vocal cord was visualized with a surgical microscope, and the lesion was carefully resected with CO2 laser. Intraoperative frozen sections confirmed clear margins. The patient was extubated without complication and transferred to the post-anesthesia care unit in stable condition. Follow-up plans include voice therapy to aid in recovery.

The patient was diagnosed with an advanced squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx, involving the left tonsillar fossa and extending into the base of tongue. A combined approach was necessary, including a transoral robotic surgery (TORS) for the primary tumor resection and a modified radical neck dissection on the left side due to clinically positive cervical nodes. The excised mass measured 4.8 cm, and the surgical margins were free of tumor on frozen section analysis. A total of 23 lymph nodes were removed, with 5 positive for metastatic disease. Postoperatively, the patient was stable and transferred to the intensive care unit for close monitoring.

The procedure involved a wide local excision of a small (1.2 cm) squamous cell carcinoma located on the floor of the mouth, adjacent to the mandibular alveolus. Due to the tumor's close proximity to the bone, periosteal stripping was performed to ensure clear margins.
