No cervical lymphadenopathy was noted on clinical examination, and thus, no neck dissection was performed. The operative site was closed with primary closure techniques. The patient was discharged on the same day with instructions for oral hygiene and follow-up.

The patient underwent a total laryngectomy for a stage IV squamous cell carcinoma of the hypopharynx. The tumor involved the pyriform sinus and extended to the post-cricoid region. A bilateral neck dissection was also performed, resulting in the removal of 42 lymph nodes, with 8 showing metastatic involvement. The pharyngeal defect was reconstructed using a pectoralis major flap. The patient was transferred to the surgical ICU for postoperative care, with plans for adjuvant chemoradiotherapy based on the extensive nodal disease.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, was brought to the operating room for a partial glossectomy due to a malignant lesion on the right lateral border of the tongue. The procedure was initiated with careful dissection to preserve critical structures, followed by resection of the 2.5 cm squamous cell carcinoma lesion while ensuring clear margins. Intraoperative frozen section analysis confirmed negative margins. A selective neck dissection was performed to remove level I-III lymph nodes due to suspicious preoperative imaging. Hemostasis was achieved, and the wound was closed in layers. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was transferred to recovery in stable condition.

A 54-year-old female with a known history of smoking presented with a progressively enlarging mass in the supraglottic region. A direct laryngoscopy was performed, revealing a 3 cm tumor involving the epiglottis. A supraglottic laryngectomy was carried out, preserving the vocal cords. The tumor was excised with adequate margins, and bilateral selective neck dissection was performed to assess potential lymphatic spread. No metastasis was detected intraoperatively. The patient was extubated successfully and transferred to the intensive care unit for postoperative monitoring.

The patient, a 68-year-old male, underwent a radical resection of an extensive oropharyngeal carcinoma involving the left tonsil and extending into the soft palate. A transoral robotic surgery (TORS) approach was utilized, allowing for precise removal of the tumor with minimal impact on surrounding tissues. Additionally, a modified radical neck dissection was performed on the left side to address clinically involved lymph nodes. Post-operative pathology confirmed a poorly differentiated squamous cell carcinoma with perineural invasion. Reconstruction was performed using a radial forearm free flap to restore palatal continuity.

A 47-year-old male presented with dysphagia and a palpable neck mass. Imaging studies revealed a hypopharyngeal tumor with involvement of the pyriform sinus. A total laryngopharyngectomy was performed, with resection of the tumor along with a bilateral neck dissection due to evidence of nodal involvement. The esophagus was reconstructed using a gastric pull-up technique. The patient required a tracheostomy postoperatively, and a feeding tube was placed for nutritional support. The operation was completed without complications, and the patient was monitored in the surgical intensive care unit.

The patient, a 59-year-old female, underwent a wide local excision of a lesion located in the floor of the mouth. The lesion, measuring 1.8 cm, was a biopsy-proven squamous cell carcinoma. The surgical approach included a mandibular swing technique to provide adequate access for complete tumor excision and reconstruction. Margins were clear on frozen section analysis. No neck dissection was performed as imaging showed no evidence of nodal involvement. The defect was closed primarily, and the patient was extubated in the operating room, showing stable vital signs.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, underwent a partial laryngectomy for a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the glottic larynx. The procedure involved the excision of the tumor with clear margins, preserving the vocal cords. Intraoperative frozen section analysis confirmed the negative margins. No lymphadenopathy was noted on pre-operative imaging, and intraoperative inspection of the neck did not reveal suspicious nodes. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was extubated post-operatively without complications. Post-operative care included speech therapy consultations to optimize vocal function.

A 55-year-old female presented with a locally advanced carcinoma of the right tonsil, classified as T3N2b. The surgery involved a right-sided tonsillectomy with concurrent modified radical neck dissection due to palpable cervical lymphadenopathy. Multiple enlarged lymph nodes were excised, and pathological evaluation confirmed metastatic involvement in three of the nodes. Reconstruction of the oropharyngeal defect was achieved using a radial forearm free flap. The patient had an uneventful recovery and was referred for adjuvant chemoradiation therapy.

The patient, a 74-year-old male, underwent an extensive resection for an advanced base of tongue squamous cell carcinoma, classified as T4a. The surgical approach included a transoral robotic-assisted resection, followed by a comprehensive bilateral neck dissection. Several ipsilateral lymph nodes were found to be metastatic, while contralateral nodes were clear. Post-operative recovery was complicated by transient dysphagia, managed with a temporary feeding tube and swallowing therapy.

A 48-year-old male diagnosed with a T1N0 squamous cell carcinoma of the buccal mucosa underwent wide local excision. The tumor was localized, and intraoperative margins were clear. No neck dissection was performed as imaging and physical examination showed no evidence of nodal involvement. The patient was discharged the following day with instructions for oral hygiene and follow-up appointments for surveillance.

The patient, a 60-year-old female, presented with a malignant neoplasm of the hypopharynx, classified as T1N1. A partial pharyngectomy was performed, and a unilateral neck dissection was conducted due to the presence of a suspicious lymph node pre-operatively. Histological assessment confirmed metastatic spread to one lymph node. Post-operative recovery was smooth, and the patient was scheduled to begin adjuvant radiotherapy.

The patient underwent a total laryngectomy with bilateral neck dissection after presenting with a T3 squamous cell carcinoma of the glottic larynx. Intraoperatively, the tumor was found to invade the thyroid cartilage but had not penetrated into the surrounding soft tissue. Bilateral level II-IV lymph nodes were dissected, with two nodes on the left side showing metastasis. The resection margins were clear. Post-operative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was referred for adjuvant radiotherapy.

A 56-year-old male presented with a large mass in the left floor of the mouth, confirmed to be squamous cell carcinoma via biopsy. A composite resection was performed, including partial mandibulectomy and left modified radical neck dissection. The tumor involved the bone but was resected with clear margins. Significant lymphadenopathy was noted, with metastatic involvement in three out of fifteen dissected nodes. Reconstruction was achieved with a fibula free flap.

The patient, a 61-year-old female, was scheduled for resection of a hypopharyngeal tumor, presenting with severe dysphagia. A total pharyngolaryngectomy was performed due to the tumor's extensive involvement of the piriform sinus and postcricoid area. A radial forearm free flap was used for reconstruction of the pharyngeal defect. All surgical margins were negative, and no lymph node metastasis was detected in the selective neck dissection.

A 47-year-old female presented with a small ulcerative lesion on the right lateral tongue, diagnosed as early-stage squamous cell carcinoma. A partial glossectomy was performed, ensuring a 1 cm margin of healthy tissue around the lesion. The neck was clinically negative, and no lymph node dissection was performed. The patient's post-operative course was uncomplicated, and she was advised regular follow-up visits for monitoring.

The patient presented with a progressively enlarging mass in the left tonsillar region, with associated cervical lymphadenopathy. A transoral robotic-assisted resection of the oropharyngeal carcinoma was performed, followed by a bilateral neck dissection. The tonsillar mass was excised with clear margins, and metastatic involvement was identified in level II and III nodes on the left side. The patient began post-operative chemoradiotherapy due to the presence of extranodal extension.

The patient presented with a T2 lesion on the right lateral wall of the oropharynx. A transoral robotic-assisted resection was performed to excise the primary tumor. The lesion was infiltrating the surrounding soft tissue but was successfully removed with clear margins. Intraoperative frozen section analysis confirmed the absence of carcinoma at the resection margins. Post-operatively, the patient required supportive care in the ICU due to transient airway edema, but recovered well. No lymph node involvement was noted upon examination of the neck dissection specimens.

A 65-year-old male was diagnosed with a T3 squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx, extending to the vocal cords and causing significant hoarseness. The patient underwent a total laryngectomy with bilateral neck dissection. Intraoperative findings included metastatic involvement of level II and III lymph nodes on the left side. The surgery proceeded uneventfully, and the patient was fitted with a tracheoesophageal puncture for voice rehabilitation. Post-surgical pathology confirmed clear margins around the primary tumor site.

The patient underwent a partial glossectomy for a well-differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the anterior two-thirds of the tongue. The tumor was limited to the mucosa without deeper tissue invasion. Sentinel lymph node biopsy was negative for metastasis. The procedure was straightforward, and the patient was discharged on postoperative day two with instructions for speech and swallowing therapy.

A 72-year-old female presented with a large ulcerative lesion at the base of the tongue, classified as T4a, with clinical evidence of invasion into the floor of the mouth. A combined approach using transmandibular and transoral access was utilized for tumor resection. The complex resection included removal of involved portions of the mandible and reconstruction with a fibular free flap. Additionally, multiple ipsilateral cervical lymph nodes were excised, with pathological analysis confirming metastatic spread. The patient was transferred to the ICU postoperatively for monitoring and management of potential airway compromise.

A 58-year-old patient presented with an exophytic mass on the left buccal mucosa. An excisional biopsy confirmed moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma. The patient underwent wide local excision of the lesion with primary closure. No evidence of regional lymphatic spread was noted on imaging and during the procedure. The surgical team ensured a 1 cm margin of healthy tissue around the excised area, and the patient was advised on regular follow-up for early detection of any recurrence.

The patient, a 65-year-old male, presented with a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the right lateral tongue. A partial glossectomy was performed with clear margins achieved. Additionally, a selective neck dissection was conducted, revealing metastasis to the ipsilateral level II lymph nodes. Reconstruction of the tongue was accomplished using a radial forearm free flap, ensuring functionality and aesthetics were maintained.

A 58-year-old female underwent a total laryngectomy for a T3 carcinoma of the glottis, which had caused significant airway obstruction. The tumor was found to invade the paraglottic space but did not extend beyond the larynx. Post-laryngectomy, a primary tracheoesophageal puncture was performed to facilitate vocal rehabilitation. No evidence of lymphatic spread was observed intraoperatively.

This 72-year-old male was diagnosed with an advanced stage oropharyngeal carcinoma involving the left tonsillar fossa. The patient underwent a transoral robotic surgery (TORS) for resection of the tumor. Margins were evaluated intraoperatively with frozen sections and found to be negative. A concurrent bilateral neck dissection was performed, with metastatic involvement found in the left level III lymph nodes.

The patient, a 50-year-old female, presented with a T2 carcinoma of the hypopharynx. A partial pharyngectomy was performed, preserving the laryngeal structures. The surgical approach included a reconstruction with a pectoralis major myocutaneous flap. Pathological examination confirmed no lymph node involvement, and the patient was scheduled for adjuvant radiotherapy.

A 60-year-old man underwent a wide local excision for a carcinoma located at the floor of the mouth. The tumor was classified as T1 with no clinical or radiological evidence of nodal metastases. The defect was reconstructed with a split-thickness skin graft. Post-operative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was discharged with instructions for regular follow-up.

The patient underwent a partial laryngectomy due to a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma located in the glottis. The tumor measured 2.5 cm and involved the left vocal cord. During surgery, careful dissection of the surrounding tissues was performed, and the tumor was excised with clear margins. No invasion into the thyroid cartilage was observed. Intraoperative frozen sections confirmed negative margins. The left level II and III cervical lymph nodes were also dissected, revealing metastatic involvement in two of the twelve nodes examined. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was extubated without complications.

The patient presented with a large exophytic mass on the right tonsil, which prompted a transoral robotic-assisted resection. The lesion was consistent with a poorly differentiated squamous cell carcinoma extending to the soft palate and base of the tongue. The surgical team achieved complete resection with clear margins, as confirmed by pathological evaluation. Given the extensive local invasion, bilateral selective neck dissection was performed, revealing no metastatic involvement. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was transferred to the recovery unit in stable condition.

The patient with a history of smoking and chronic alcohol use presented with a lesion in the floor of the mouth. After thorough evaluation, a wide local excision was performed to remove a 1.8 cm moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma. The tumor was confined to the mucosal layer without evidence of bone invasion. A sentinel lymph node biopsy was conducted, with all nodes returning negative for metastatic disease. The patient experienced no immediate postoperative complications and was discharged with instructions for follow-up care.

The patient underwent a total glossectomy for a 4 cm invasive squamous cell carcinoma of the tongue, which was causing significant dysphagia and speech difficulties. The tumor had infiltrated the intrinsic muscles of the tongue but spared the extrinsic muscles. Extensive reconstruction was performed using a radial forearm free flap to restore continuity and function. In addition, a bilateral modified radical neck dissection was carried out, revealing metastatic disease in level II and III nodes on the right side. The patient was monitored in the intensive care unit postoperatively and began speech therapy early in the recovery period.

A patient with a rapidly enlarging mass in the left submandibular region underwent surgical excision of a 3 cm squamous cell carcinoma originating in the retromolar trigone. The tumor extended into the adjacent buccal mucosa and involved the mandibular periosteum, necessitating segmental mandibulectomy. Margins were clear upon final pathological assessment. A left neck dissection was performed, which was negative for metastatic spread. The patient was stable postoperatively and was referred to oncology for further adjuvant therapy consideration.

The patient, a 58-year-old male, presented with a T2N0M0 squamous cell carcinoma of the glottic larynx. The surgical procedure involved a partial laryngectomy to remove the tumor localized in the glottis, preserving the uninvolved structures of the larynx. Intraoperative frozen sections confirmed negative margins. No lymphadenopathy was noted during the procedure. The patient tolerated the surgery well with no immediate postoperative complications.

A 62-year-old female with a history of smoking presented with a T3N2bM0 carcinoma of the oropharynx, specifically involving the base of the tongue. The surgical team performed a transoral robotic surgery (TORS) for tumor excision, followed by a modified radical neck dissection on the left side due to palpable lymphadenopathy. Multiple lymph nodes were excised and sent for pathological examination, confirming metastatic involvement. The procedure was completed successfully, and the patient was transferred to the recovery unit in stable condition.

The patient, a 70-year-old male, was diagnosed with a T1N0M0 squamous cell carcinoma of the floor of the mouth. Excision of the primary tumor was performed with wide local excision margins, ensuring complete removal of the lesion. The submandibular gland was spared as there was no evidence of spread beyond the primary site. The operation was uneventful, and the patient demonstrated good recovery postoperatively.

A 55-year-old female presented with a T4aN1M0 carcinoma of the hypopharynx with extension to the surrounding structures. The surgical approach included a total laryngopharyngectomy with bilateral selective neck dissection. Multiple lymph nodes were found to be involved on the right side. Reconstruction was accomplished using a pectoralis major myocutaneous flap. The patient experienced minor postoperative bleeding, which was managed conservatively.

A 48-year-old male with a history of HPV-positive status was diagnosed with a T2N0M0 squamous cell carcinoma of the tonsil. The tumor was resected using transoral laser microsurgery, achieving clear margins. No neck dissection was performed as there was no clinical or radiological evidence of lymph node involvement. The patient was discharged with instructions for follow-up and adjuvant therapy considerations.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, underwent a partial laryngectomy due to a T2 tumor located on the left vocal cord. During the procedure, the tumor was excised with clear margins, and a frozen section confirmed the absence of malignancy at the resection edges. The surrounding tissue was examined for possible microscopic spread, but none was identified. The patient had no palpable cervical lymphadenopathy, and an intraoperative decision was made not to proceed with a neck dissection. The airway was maintained with a temporary tracheostomy, and postoperative recovery was uneventful.

A 58-year-old female presented with a squamous cell carcinoma of the right tonsil. The tumor had invaded the surrounding soft palate and extended to the base of the tongue. A transoral robotic surgery (TORS) was performed to resect the tumor, followed by a right-sided selective neck dissection due to clinically suspicious lymph nodes. Intraoperative pathology revealed metastatic involvement in two of the dissected nodes. The patient was closely monitored for postoperative complications and began adjuvant radiation therapy.

The patient, a 70-year-old male with a history of tobacco use, presented with a poorly differentiated carcinoma of the hypopharynx. The tumor extended into the pyriform sinus on the left side. A total pharyngolaryngectomy with bilateral neck dissection was performed. The procedure included reconstruction using a radial forearm free flap to restore the pharyngeal defect. Pathology confirmed the presence of metastatic deposits in the left neck nodes. The patient is scheduled for postoperative chemoradiation.

A 45-year-old male underwent a wide local excision of a T1 squamous cell carcinoma of the buccal mucosa. The lesion was located on the right cheek and excised with 1 cm margins. No lymph node involvement was suspected, and therefore, no neck dissection was performed. The surgical site was closed primarily, and the patient was advised on oral hygiene to prevent postoperative infection. The patient was discharged the following day with a follow-up in one week.

The patient, a 53-year-old female, was diagnosed with a carcinoma of the floor of the mouth. A composite resection involving partial mandibulectomy and floor of the mouth resection was performed. In addition, a modified radical neck dissection was conducted on the affected side due to enlarged cervical lymph nodes. Intraoperative frozen sections confirmed clear surgical margins and the presence of metastatic carcinoma in the cervical nodes. The patient is to receive adjuvant therapy.

The patient underwent a partial glossectomy due to a well-demarcated squamous cell carcinoma located on the left lateral aspect of the tongue. The tumor measured approximately 2.5 cm in diameter and was excised with a 1 cm margin of healthy tissue. Intraoperative frozen section analysis confirmed clear margins. A selective neck dissection was also performed, removing levels I-III lymph nodes on the left side. Pathological examination revealed metastatic involvement in two lymph nodes at level II.

The surgical team performed a total laryngectomy on a patient diagnosed with a T3 squamous cell carcinoma of the glottic larynx. The tumor had caused significant airway obstruction and vocal cord immobility. The procedure included removal of the entire larynx and creation of a permanent stoma. A bilateral neck dissection was conducted, which revealed no lymph node metastasis. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was planned for adjuvant radiotherapy.

A transoral robotic surgery (TORS) was executed to resect a base of tongue cancer, which was identified as a T1 moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma. The tumor measured 1.5 cm and was confined to the base of the tongue without extension to adjacent structures. Margins were clear upon pathological examination. No neck dissection was performed as preoperative imaging showed no evidence of lymph node involvement.

The patient presented with a T4a carcinoma of the maxillary sinus, resulting in invasion into the hard palate and the anterior wall of the maxilla. A maxillectomy was performed, along with reconstruction using a free flap from the radial forearm. The surgery included removal of the affected portion of the maxilla to achieve clear margins. Pathological evaluation showed perineural invasion but no nodal involvement was detected in the elective neck dissection.

A composite resection was performed for a patient with recurrent squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx, initially treated with chemoradiotherapy. The tumor involved the left tonsillar region and extended into the soft palate. The surgical team carried out a wide local excision, including parts of the soft palate and tonsillar fossa. A concurrent modified radical neck dissection was performed on the left side, which revealed metastatic involvement of level II and III lymph nodes.

The patient, a 62-year-old male with a history of tobacco use, presented with a squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. A total laryngectomy was performed, and the tumor was found to be invading the thyroid cartilage without evidence of extralaryngeal spread. Margins were clear, and no perineural invasion was observed. The patient also underwent a bilateral neck dissection, revealing metastasis to a single ipsilateral lymph node. Reconstruction was achieved using a radial forearm free flap, and the patient tolerated the procedure well.

A 55-year-old female with a poorly differentiated carcinoma of the base of the tongue was admitted for surgical resection. A transoral robotic surgery was performed to excise the primary tumor, which measured 3.5 cm and was infiltrating the lingual muscles. The surgical margins were negative. Additionally, a modified radical neck dissection was conducted which revealed metastatic involvement in two cervical lymph nodes. Postoperative recovery was uneventful.

A 70-year-old male with a recurrent squamous cell carcinoma in the left tonsillar region underwent a wide local excision and partial pharyngectomy. The resection included the tonsil and surrounding oropharyngeal tissues. Intraoperative frozen sections confirmed clear margins. No lymphadenopathy was evident during neck exploration. The surgical site was closed primarily, and the patient was extubated without complication.

The patient, a 48-year-old female, presented with a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the right buccal mucosa. A wide local excision with 1 cm margins was performed, and the defect was reconstructed using a split-thickness skin graft. Examination of the excised tissue showed no lymphovascular invasion. A sentinel lymph node biopsy was negative for malignancy. The patient was discharged on postoperative day two in stable condition.

A 60-year-old male with a carcinoma of the hypopharynx underwent a partial pharyngectomy. The tumor, located in the piriform sinus, was resected with negative margins. A selective neck dissection was simultaneously performed which yielded no evidence of metastasis in the lymph nodes. An anterolateral thigh flap was utilized for reconstruction. The patient was monitored in the ICU postoperatively and showed a good recovery trajectory.

The patient underwent a partial glossectomy due to a squamous cell carcinoma located in the left lateral border of the tongue. The tumor measured 2.3 cm in greatest dimension and was predominantly exophytic with some infiltration into the surrounding muscle tissue. Margins were biopsied and sent for frozen section analysis, confirming clear margins of 1.0 cm. As part of the procedure, a selective neck dissection was performed, revealing metastatic involvement of the ipsilateral level II lymph nodes. The surgical site was closed primarily, and the patient was transferred to the recovery room in stable condition.

A total laryngectomy was conducted on a 62-year-old male with a history of heavy smoking, presenting with a T3 tumor of the glottic larynx. The tumor extended to the anterior commissure but did not invade the thyroid cartilage. The surgical procedure included the removal of the entire larynx and the creation of a tracheostomy to ensure an adequate airway. Bilateral neck dissection was performed, and no metastatic disease was found in the lymph nodes. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was extubated without complications.

The patient, a 55-year-old female, underwent a radical resection of the right buccal mucosa due to a poorly differentiated carcinoma. The tumor involved the full thickness of the buccal mucosa and extended into the buccinator muscle. A reconstruction was performed using a radial forearm free flap to restore oral cavity function. Intraoperative examination of the neck revealed multiple enlarged nodes; however, only one level I node showed metastatic disease upon histopathological examination. The patient was monitored postoperatively in the surgical intensive care unit.

A transoral robotic-assisted surgery was performed on a 47-year-old male with a base of tongue carcinoma. The lesion, measuring 1.5 cm, was confined to the right side and did not cross the midline. The procedure involved resection of the tumor with clear surgical margins, as confirmed by intraoperative pathology. No lymph node dissection was deemed necessary as preoperative imaging showed no evidence of nodal involvement. The robotic approach allowed for excellent visualization and precision, minimizing postoperative recovery time.

The patient presented with a mass in the hypopharynx and underwent a partial pharyngectomy. The tumor was located in the pyriform sinus and measured 3.0 cm. Margins were clear, with no evidence of invasion into adjacent structures. A comprehensive neck dissection showed metastatic disease in three ipsilateral cervical lymph nodes, which were excised. The patient was advised regarding the need for adjuvant therapy given the nodal involvement. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was discharged on postoperative day five.

The patient, a 65-year-old male, underwent a partial laryngectomy due to a biopsy-confirmed squamous cell carcinoma of the glottis. Intraoperatively, the tumor was localized to the anterior two-thirds of the right vocal cord, with no visible extension to the supraglottic or subglottic regions. A frozen section analysis confirmed clear margins. No lymphatic involvement was noted upon examination of the regional nodes. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was extubated post-surgery with an expected hoarseness.

A 54-year-old female presented with a T3 carcinoma of the oropharynx, specifically originating from the base of the tongue. She underwent a transoral robotic surgery (TORS) for tumor resection. The tumor had invaded the adjacent lingual tonsil area, necessitating a partial glossectomy. Concurrent modified radical neck dissection was performed on the right side due to palpable metastatic lymph nodes. The surgical team achieved negative margins, and pathology later confirmed metastatic involvement in two of the resected nodes.

The patient, a 47-year-old male, underwent a composite resection for a large squamous cell carcinoma of the left buccal mucosa, extending into the retromolar trigone. Given the tumor's size and location, a segmental mandibulectomy was necessary, followed by immediate reconstruction using a fibula free flap. Perineural invasion was noted, but no lymph node metastases were found in the level I-III nodes dissected. The surgery was completed without complications.

A 72-year-old female with a history of smoking presented with a carcinoma of the hypopharynx. The surgical team performed a total laryngopharyngectomy due to extensive tumor spread involving the piriform sinus and postcricoid area. Bilateral neck dissection was also conducted. Multiple metastatic nodes were identified and excised from the left cervical region. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, with a plan for adjuvant radiotherapy.

A 60-year-old male underwent an excision of a small, well-differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the hard palate. The tumor was confined to the mucosal surface with no evidence of bone invasion. Margins were assessed intraoperatively, and clear margins were achieved. No lymphadenopathy was observed, and the patient was discharged with instructions for follow-up to monitor for recurrence.

The patient underwent a total laryngectomy for a T3 squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. The tumor involved the right vocal cord with extension into the subglottic region. Surgical margins were negative. A right-sided neck dissection was also performed, revealing metastatic carcinoma in two of the dissected lymph nodes. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was transferred to recovery in stable condition.

A partial glossectomy was performed on a patient with a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the left lateral tongue. The lesion measured 2.5 cm and was resected with clear margins. No evidence of invasion into the floor of the mouth was noted. The procedure was completed without complications, and the patient was extubated and transferred to the postoperative unit.

The patient presented with a tumor of the oropharynx involving the tonsillar region. A transoral robotic-assisted resection of the tumor was carried out. Intraoperative frozen sections confirmed negative margins. Postoperative pathology revealed a T1 lesion with no lymphatic involvement. The patient was stable post-surgery and required minimal postoperative care.

The patient was diagnosed with an advanced carcinoma of the hypopharynx, necessitating a total pharyngolaryngectomy. The tumor was invading the pyriform sinus and extending to the postcricoid area. Bilateral neck dissections were performed, and metastatic spread was identified in the left cervical lymph nodes. The operation was extensive but uneventful, and the patient was transferred to the ICU for close monitoring.

A wide local excision of a buccal mucosa carcinoma was performed for a small, well-differentiated squamous cell carcinoma measuring 1.2 cm. The tumor was superficial, with no invasion into the underlying musculature. Margins were confirmed negative by intraoperative pathology consultation. The surgery proceeded without incident, and the patient demonstrated satisfactory recovery.

The patient, a 65-year-old male, presented with a mass in the right side of the neck and hoarseness. Upon examination, a 3 cm tumor was identified on the right true vocal cord, extending to the anterior commissure. The patient underwent a partial laryngectomy with preservation of the left vocal cord. Intraoperatively, the tumor was found to infiltrate the thyroarytenoid muscle. A selective neck dissection was performed, revealing metastatic involvement in one out of ten right cervical lymph nodes.

A 58-year-old female was diagnosed with a large ulcerative lesion on the left lateral border of the tongue. The lesion measured 4 cm and was suspected to invade the floor of the mouth. The patient underwent a hemiglossectomy and reconstruction with a radial forearm free flap. The surgical margins were clear, and no lymphatic spread was observed. The surgery was successful, with no complications.

The patient, a 72-year-old male, presented with dysphagia and weight loss. Imaging revealed a 5 cm tumor in the hypopharynx, extending to the cervical esophagus. A total laryngopharyngectomy was performed, with esophageal reconstruction using a gastric pull-up procedure. In addition, bilateral modified radical neck dissections were conducted, which confirmed bilateral lymph node involvement.

A 49-year-old male with a history of smoking presented with a non-healing ulcer on the soft palate. Biopsy confirmed squamous cell carcinoma. The patient underwent a transoral robotic excision of the soft palate tumor. The defect was closed with a local palatal flap. No evidence of regional metastasis was found, and the surgical margins were negative for tumor involvement.

The patient, a 62-year-old female, was referred for a rapidly enlarging mass in the left tonsillar region. A thorough examination revealed a 4.5 cm tumor with ipsilateral cervical lymphadenopathy. The patient underwent a left tonsillectomy with radical neck dissection. Pathology confirmed poorly differentiated squamous cell carcinoma with three out of fifteen lymph nodes positive for metastasis.

The patient, a 58-year-old male, underwent a partial laryngectomy for a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the left true vocal cord. Intraoperatively, the tumor was found to be extending into the anterior commissure, but there was no involvement of the thyroid cartilage. Margins were obtained with clear edges, and a selective neck dissection of levels II-IV on the left side was performed, revealing metastatic involvement in two of the six lymph nodes excised.

A 63-year-old female presented with a rapidly growing mass in the oral cavity. A wide local excision of a T1 squamous cell carcinoma on the right side of the floor of the mouth was performed. The mass was excised with a 1 cm margin, and frozen sections confirmed negative margins. No lymphadenopathy was noted, and no neck dissection was performed.

The patient, a 72-year-old male, underwent a total glossectomy with bilateral neck dissection for a T3 squamous cell carcinoma of the tongue. The tumor had extensively infiltrated the tongue musculature but was confined to the tongue without crossing the midline. Bilateral neck dissection showed metastasis in the right level II lymph nodes, but the left side was clear.

A 45-year-old male with a history of smoking presented with dysphagia and odynophagia. He underwent transoral robotic surgery for a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the base of the tongue. The procedure included resection of the tumor with negative margins, and a concurrent right side selective neck dissection was performed, which was negative for metastatic disease.

The patient, a 65-year-old female, underwent a radical neck dissection followed by a partial pharyngectomy for a T4a squamous cell carcinoma of the hypopharynx involving the pyriform sinus. The tumor had invaded the prevertebral fascia, and multiple lymph nodes were positive for metastasis. The resection margins were clear after extensive resection.

The patient underwent a partial glossectomy due to a lesion located on the anterior two-thirds of the tongue. The tumor, measuring approximately 2 cm in diameter, was excised with clear margins. Intraoperative frozen section confirmed negative margins. The procedure included a selective neck dissection on the left side to assess for potential lymphatic spread. Three lymph nodes were removed and sent for pathological evaluation, which later confirmed metastatic involvement.

The surgical team performed a total laryngectomy on the patient, who presented with a T3 squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. This extensive surgery involved the removal of the entire larynx and surrounding tissues, including the pre-epiglottic space. A tracheostomy was created for airway management. Postoperative pathology confirmed the tumor's involvement of the thyroid cartilage but no lymph node metastasis was detected in the central compartment dissection.

The patient, diagnosed with carcinoma of the oropharynx, underwent a transoral robotic-assisted resection of a tumor located in the base of the tongue. The tumor was approximately 3.5 cm in size with bilateral tonsillar involvement. A bilateral neck dissection was performed, and microscopic examination revealed metastatic deposits in multiple ipsilateral lymph nodes.

Surgical intervention involved a composite resection of a carcinoma situated on the floor of the mouth. The excision included part of the mandible due to tumor invasion. Reconstruction was performed with a fibula free flap to restore mandibular continuity. Pathology reported clear surgical margins, and no regional lymphatic spread was observed.

The patient presented with a malignant neoplasm of the hypopharynx, necessitating a partial pharyngectomy with reconstruction using a radial forearm free flap. The tumor extended to the pyriform sinus but was contained without extrinsic muscle invasion. A selective neck dissection was conducted, and histopathological examination showed no evidence of nodal metastasis.

The patient underwent a total laryngectomy for a squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx, involving the glottic region. The tumor was staged as T3 due to local extension beyond the vocal cords with fixation. In addition, a right neck dissection was performed to address a suspicious level II lymph node. Intraoperatively, the tumor was found to have invaded the thyroid cartilage. Post-surgical margins were clear, and the lymph node was confirmed to contain metastatic carcinoma on frozen section analysis. The procedure was completed without complications, and the patient was transferred to the recovery room in stable condition.

The patient presented with a large exophytic lesion on the left lateral tongue, which was biopsied and confirmed to be a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma. A partial glossectomy was performed, with careful attention to preserve as much function as possible. The tumor had invaded the underlying muscle but was confined to the tongue without evidence of regional spread. Surgical margins were negative, and no lymphadenopathy was detected on imaging or during surgery.

This patient underwent a transoral robotic resection of an oropharyngeal carcinoma, located primarily in the base of the tongue. The tumor was staged as T2N1, with one palpable ipsilateral level II lymph node that was also excised. The primary lesion was successfully resected with clear margins, although it did extend close to the midline. The lymph node dissection confirmed metastatic involvement. Post-operatively, the patient was extubated and monitored in the intensive care unit for airway patency.

A radical neck dissection was performed for a patient with a diagnosis of hypopharyngeal carcinoma. The primary lesion was situated in the piriform sinus and demonstrated significant extension into the postcricoid area. Multiple enlarged cervical lymph nodes were identified pre-operatively on imaging, suggesting advanced nodal disease. The surgical approach included resection of involved tissues and comprehensive lymphadenectomy. Pathology confirmed extensive lymph node metastasis, consistent with advanced stage disease. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was admitted to the surgical ward post-operatively.

The patient underwent wide excision of a malignant lesion within the buccal mucosa. The tumor was small, measuring approximately 1.5 cm, and was classified as a T1 lesion with no clinical or radiological evidence of nodal involvement. Surrounding tissues were excised with a generous margin to ensure complete removal, and frozen sections were used intraoperatively to confirm negative margins. The patient had an uneventful recovery post-surgery and was discharged on the same day.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, underwent a partial glossectomy due to a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the left lateral border of the tongue. The tumor measured 3.2 cm and was confined to the oral cavity without evidence of invasion into the floor of the mouth. A selective neck dissection was performed, and 18 lymph nodes were excised, all of which were negative for metastatic disease. The surgical margins were clear, and the recovery was uneventful.

A 55-year-old female presented with a locally advanced carcinoma of the supraglottic larynx. A total laryngectomy was performed, accompanied by a right-sided modified radical neck dissection because of clinically palpable lymphadenopathy. The tumor involved the epiglottis and extended to the right aryepiglottic fold. Intraoperatively, the tumor was found to be invading through the thyroid cartilage. Six out of 24 lymph nodes were positive for metastatic carcinoma.

This 47-year-old male with a history of tobacco use underwent a radical resection of the oropharynx for a T3 tumor involving the base of the tongue and extending into the tonsillar fossa. In addition, a bilateral neck dissection was performed. The pathologic examination revealed positive margins anteriorly, and 3 out of 15 lymph nodes on the left side were positive for metastasis.

The patient, a 70-year-old female, presented with a painful mass in the left submandibular region. Imaging studies suggested a malignant tumor of the salivary gland. A complete excision of the left submandibular gland was performed, revealing an adenocarcinoma. No lymph nodes were involved. Postoperatively, the patient recovered well with minimal complications.

A 60-year-old male with a history of chronic alcohol use was diagnosed with a squamous cell carcinoma of the hypopharynx. A total pharyngolaryngectomy was executed along with a left-sided neck dissection. The tumor extended to the pyriform sinus and involved the lateral wall of the hypopharynx. Pathology revealed involvement of the resection margins and metastasis in 2 out of 10 lymph nodes.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, presented with a T2N0M0 squamous cell carcinoma of the left vocal cord. The surgical procedure involved a partial laryngectomy to excise the tumor while preserving voice function. Intraoperative frozen section analysis confirmed negative margins. The surgery proceeded without complications, and no lymphadenopathy was noted during the procedure. The patient was extubated successfully and transferred to the recovery unit in stable condition.

A 58-year-old female with a history of smoking presented with a T3N2bM0 carcinoma of the right tonsil. The surgical approach included a transoral robotic-assisted resection of the oropharyngeal tumor, followed by a right modified radical neck dissection. Multiple ipsilateral lymph nodes were found to be metastatic. The surgery was uneventful, and the patient was monitored post-operatively in the intensive care unit for airway management.

The patient, a 45-year-old male, was diagnosed with a T1N0M0 mucoepidermoid carcinoma of the minor salivary glands located on the anterior floor of the mouth. A wide local excision was performed with clear margins, and no lymph node involvement was detected. The procedure was uncomplicated, and the patient was discharged with instructions for follow-up care and oral hygiene management.

A 70-year-old male with a long-standing history of alcohol use was diagnosed with T2N1M0 squamous cell carcinoma of the hypopharynx. The surgical team performed a total laryngopharyngectomy with bilateral selective neck dissection. The procedure revealed metastatic involvement of the right cervical lymph nodes. The patient was fitted with a tracheostomy post-surgery and transferred to the ICU for continued observation and respiratory support.

The patient, a 54-year-old female, presented with a T4aN0M0 squamous cell carcinoma involving the maxillary sinus. A combined approach using endoscopic and open techniques was utilized for maxillectomy, achieving complete tumor resection with negative margins. No lymph node involvement was identified. The patient underwent reconstruction of the surgical defect using a free flap and was transferred to the surgical ward in stable condition.

The patient underwent a partial glossectomy for a lesion on the anterior two-thirds of the tongue, identified as squamous cell carcinoma. The lesion was approximately 2.5 cm in diameter with clear margins achieved. A selective neck dissection was performed, removing levels I-III lymph nodes due to the presence of a suspicious node. The lymph node was confirmed to be positive for metastatic carcinoma. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was discharged with instructions for follow-up care in oncology.

A total laryngectomy was performed on a patient diagnosed with advanced laryngeal carcinoma. The tumor involved the vocal cords and extended to the subglottic region, necessitating complete removal of the larynx. Bilateral neck dissection was conducted, revealing metastatic spread to multiple cervical lymph nodes. A tracheostomy was established, and the patient was advised on rehabilitation for speech and swallowing functions post-surgery.

The patient presented with a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the left tonsil. A transoral robotic surgery (TORS) was conducted to excise the primary tumor. Margins were clear, and no lymphadenopathy was observed on imaging, hence no neck dissection was performed. Postoperative healing was satisfactory, and adjuvant radiotherapy was recommended due to the tumor's T3 classification.

A wide local excision was performed on a patient with a carcinoma of the floor of the mouth. The surgery included removal of the lesion along with a margin of normal tissue. Pathology confirmed a well-differentiated squamous cell carcinoma with no perineural invasion. A sentinel lymph node biopsy was negative, eliminating the need for further neck dissection. The patient was advised on oral hygiene and regular follow-up.

The patient underwent an extensive resection involving the hypopharynx due to an infiltrative squamous cell carcinoma. The tumor was located in the piriform sinus and extended to the postcricoid area. A reconstructive flap was used to repair the surgical defect. Bilateral selective neck dissection revealed metastases in multiple nodes, and adjuvant chemoradiation was planned. The patient was monitored closely in the ICU postoperatively.

The patient, a 58-year-old male, presented with a T2 tumor of the left vocal cord. A direct laryngoscopy was performed revealing a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma. A partial laryngectomy was initiated, excising the affected portion of the left vocal cord while preserving the right side for phonation. Margins were clear upon intraoperative frozen section analysis. The patient tolerated the procedure well with no immediate complications.

A 62-year-old female was diagnosed with locally advanced carcinoma of the base of the tongue. A transoral robotic approach was chosen for resection. The tumor involved the right side, extending to the vallecula. A right-sided modified radical neck dissection was performed due to palpable lymphadenopathy. Pathology confirmed metastatic involvement of two lymph nodes. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was scheduled for adjuvant radiotherapy.

The patient, a 45-year-old male, presented with a mass in the floor of the mouth, confirmed as squamous cell carcinoma through biopsy. A wide local excision was performed with reconstruction using a radial forearm free flap. The tumor was invading into the mandible, necessitating a marginal mandibulectomy. No lymph node dissection was needed as preoperative imaging showed no evidence of nodal metastasis. The patient was extubated post-procedure and transferred to the recovery unit in stable condition.

A 70-year-old male was referred for a rapidly enlarging neck mass. Examination and imaging studies revealed a primary tumor in the piriform sinus with extensive involvement of the hypopharynx and bilateral cervical lymphadenopathy. A total laryngopharyngectomy with bilateral neck dissection was performed. The procedure was lengthy due to the need for pharyngeal reconstruction using a jejunal free flap. Postoperative care included intensive monitoring in the ICU with gradual improvement.

A 50-year-old female presented with a non-healing ulcer on the buccal mucosa. Biopsy revealed invasive squamous cell carcinoma. The surgical team performed an excision of the lesion with a 1 cm margin. To address potential lymphatic spread, a selective neck dissection was conducted on the ipsilateral side. Histological examination showed no nodal involvement. The patient recovered well and was discharged with instructions for follow-up in the outpatient clinic.

The patient underwent a partial glossectomy for a T2 squamous cell carcinoma located on the lateral border of the tongue. The lesion measured approximately 2.5 cm in greatest dimension. Margins were taken circumferentially, with frozen section analysis confirming clear resection margins. No perineural invasion was noted. A selective neck dissection was also performed, removing levels I-III lymph nodes due to the presence of clinically suspicious nodes. Pathology later confirmed metastatic involvement in two of the nodes.

The surgical team performed a total laryngectomy on a patient diagnosed with stage III squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx, specifically involving the glottic region. The procedure included resection of the larynx along with bilateral neck dissection. Intraoperatively, the tumor was observed to infiltrate the thyroid cartilage, necessitating its removal. Post-operative pathology indicated negative lymph nodes and clear surgical margins.

A 59-year-old patient presented with a large mass in the oropharynx, identified as a T4 squamous cell carcinoma involving the base of the tongue and extending to the soft palate. A transoral robotic surgery (TORS) approach was chosen to resect the tumor, given its accessibility and the patient's anatomy. A concurrent ipsilateral neck dissection was carried out, with metastasis found in one of the dissected lymph nodes. Reconstruction of the oropharyngeal defect was performed using a radial forearm free flap.

The patient, diagnosed with a T1 carcinoma of the floor of the mouth, underwent a wide local excision of the lesion, which was less than 2 cm in size. The surgical team achieved negative margins, and no lymphatic spread was observed clinically or radiologically. Due to the early stage and favorable features, a neck dissection was deemed unnecessary.

A patient with a history of alcohol and tobacco use presented with a T3 squamous cell carcinoma of the hypopharynx. A total pharyngolaryngectomy was performed, along with reconstruction using a gastric pull-up technique due to extensive tissue involvement. Bilateral neck dissection was also conducted, revealing multiple metastatic nodes on the left side. Post-operative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was referred for adjuvant radiotherapy.

The patient underwent a partial laryngectomy to address a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the right vocal cord. Intraoperative findings confirmed the tumor was localized to the true vocal cord without extension to the supraglottic or subglottic regions. Margins were clear upon frozen section analysis. No lymphadenopathy was observed during the procedure, and no additional neck dissection was required. The patient was extubated successfully in the operating room and transferred to recovery in stable condition.

The surgical team performed a composite resection involving the oral cavity and oropharynx to excise a poorly differentiated carcinoma of the base of the tongue. The tumor had infiltrated the lingual tonsil and extended into the surrounding soft tissue. A neck dissection was conducted, revealing metastatic involvement of the ipsilateral cervical lymph nodes. Reconstruction was achieved using a radial forearm free flap. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was admitted to the ICU for postoperative monitoring.

A total glossectomy was conducted for a patient presenting with an extensive squamous cell carcinoma of the anterior two-thirds of the tongue, with invasion into the floor of the mouth. The tumor was resected with clear margins, and bilateral neck dissection was performed, showing positive nodes on the left side. The surgical defect was reconstructed with an anterolateral thigh flap. The patient was hemodynamically stable post-surgery and transferred to the surgical ward.

The patient underwent a transoral robotic surgery (TORS) for resection of a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the left tonsil. The tumor was confined to the tonsillar fossa with no evidence of spread to adjacent structures. A selective neck dissection was carried out, which did not reveal any metastatic lymph node involvement. The patient had an uneventful recovery and was advised on postoperative care and follow-up.

A patient with a malignant neoplasm of the right maxillary sinus underwent a maxillectomy with orbital preservation. The tumor had eroded into the hard palate, requiring resection of involved bone. The surgical margins were clear of carcinoma. A split-thickness skin graft was used to cover the defect. The operation included an elective neck dissection, which showed no lymph node metastases. The patient was monitored in the recovery room before being moved to the oncology floor.

The patient presented with a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. A partial laryngectomy was performed, involving the removal of the left vocal cord and adjacent tissues. Intraoperative frozen section analysis confirmed clear margins. The procedure included a tracheostomy to secure the airway post-operatively. There was no evidence of local lymph node metastasis, and reconstruction was completed using local tissue flaps to preserve function.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, was diagnosed with a stage III carcinoma of the oropharynx, involving the base of the tongue and extending to the tonsillar fossa. A transoral robotic surgery (TORS) was conducted to excise the tumor. Bilateral neck dissection was performed due to clinical suspicion of nodal involvement, and metastatic disease was confirmed in the left cervical lymph nodes. The surgical plan included reconstruction with a radial forearm free flap.

An 80-year-old female with a history of tobacco use presented with a lesion in the right floor of the mouth. Biopsy confirmed a T1N0M0 squamous cell carcinoma. The surgical intervention involved a local excision with clear margins achieved. No lymphadenopathy was observed, and the postoperative course was uneventful. The patient was advised on smoking cessation and follow-up care.

The patient, a 55-year-old woman, was found to have a malignant tumor in the hypopharynx, classified as T3N1M0. A total laryngopharyngectomy was performed, along with a selective neck dissection due to palpable lymph nodes. Pathology confirmed metastatic involvement of the cervical nodes. A jejunal free flap was used for reconstruction of the pharyngeal defect, and a permanent tracheostomy was established.

A 67-year-old male underwent surgery for a large mass in the oral cavity. The tumor involved the gingiva and extended to the buccal mucosa. A composite resection including partial mandibulectomy was required due to bone invasion. Intraoperative assessment showed no regional lymph node involvement. Reconstruction was achieved using an osteocutaneous fibula flap to restore mandibular continuity and oral function.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, presented with a mass in the left side of the neck and a hoarse voice. Upon examination and imaging, a tumor was identified in the left false vocal cord extending into the supraglottic larynx. A direct laryngoscopy and biopsy confirmed squamous cell carcinoma. The patient underwent a total laryngectomy with bilateral neck dissection. The surgery was uneventful, and all visible tumor tissue was excised. Pathology confirmed the presence of metastatic squamous cell carcinoma in two of the left-sided cervical lymph nodes.

A 48-year-old female presented with a non-healing ulcer on the left lateral border of the tongue. Biopsy results indicated squamous cell carcinoma. A partial glossectomy was performed, along with selective neck dissection to remove at-risk lymph nodes. Margins were clear on frozen section analysis. No lymph node metastasis was identified in the dissected nodes, and the patient is recovering well post-operation.

The patient, a 55-year-old male, was diagnosed with an exophytic mass in the right tonsil with ipsilateral cervical lymphadenopathy. A transoral robotic-assisted resection of the right tonsillar tumor was performed, followed by a right modified radical neck dissection. The surgical team successfully excised the tumor, and pathology reported involvement of two of the ipsilateral cervical lymph nodes.

A 70-year-old male with a history of heavy tobacco use presented with dysphagia and weight loss. Imaging revealed a large mass in the hypopharynx. The patient underwent a partial pharyngectomy with reconstruction using a radial forearm free flap. Intraoperative findings confirmed extensive tumor invasion into the musculature, which was resected with clear margins. Postoperative recovery was complicated by minor flap congestion, which resolved with conservative management.

A 60-year-old female presented with an indurated lesion on the floor of the mouth. A biopsy confirmed the presence of squamous cell carcinoma. The patient underwent a composite resection of the floor of mouth tumor with marginal mandibulectomy and reconstruction using a split-thickness skin graft. There was no evidence of lymphatic spread, and the surgical margins were clear of tumor.

The patient underwent a partial laryngectomy due to a T2 stage squamous cell carcinoma located on the left true vocal cord. Intraoperatively, the tumor was found to be confined to the vocal cord with no extension to the surrounding structures. Margins were clear after resection. No regional lymphadenopathy was noted upon examination, and no neck dissection was required. The patient was extubated successfully and transferred to recovery in stable condition.

A 63-year-old male presented with a large ulcerative lesion on the lateral border of the tongue. A wide local excision of the lesion was performed with clear margins, and a selective neck dissection was carried out, given the presence of palpable lymph nodes. Pathology confirmed the presence of a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the tongue with metastasis to one ipsilateral cervical lymph node. Post-operatively, the patient was monitored closely and showed no signs of complications.

Surgical resection was conducted for a patient with a known diagnosis of carcinoma of the hypopharynx, specifically involving the piriform sinus. A total laryngopharyngectomy was performed, along with a bilateral neck dissection due to palpable lymph nodes on both sides of the neck. The tumor was found to invade the surrounding muscle and cartilage, necessitating a complex reconstruction. Multiple lymph nodes were positive for metastatic disease. The patient required a temporary tracheostomy post-operatively.

The patient underwent a transoral robotic surgery (TORS) for a small base of tongue carcinoma. The tumor was confined to the base of the tongue with no visible spread to adjacent structures. The procedure was successful with clear surgical margins achieved. There was no evidence of cervical lymphadenopathy clinically, and no neck dissection was performed. The patient was extubated without issue and transferred to the surgical ward.

A complex surgical procedure was performed involving the resection of a large oropharyngeal tumor affecting the tonsillar region. The patient had a history of rapidly enlarging neck masses, prompting a comprehensive neck dissection. Multiple lymph nodes were positive for metastatic involvement. The primary tumor was resected with clear margins, and reconstruction involved a radial forearm free flap. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was transferred to intensive care for post-operative monitoring.

The patient, a 58-year-old male, presented with a squamous cell carcinoma of the right lateral tongue. The tumor measured 3.2 cm in greatest dimension and was infiltrating into the underlying muscle tissue. A partial glossectomy was performed, with careful preservation of the lingual nerve. The surgical margins were clear, with a minimum distance of 5 mm. Additionally, a right-sided neck dissection was conducted, revealing metastatic involvement of two out of twelve lymph nodes. The postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was advised to follow up with adjuvant radiotherapy.

A 65-year-old female patient was diagnosed with carcinoma of the larynx, specifically affecting the glottis. The tumor was classified as T2 due to vocal cord fixation. A total laryngectomy was performed, and the surrounding tissues were meticulously examined for any signs of further invasion. The procedure included the creation of a tracheoesophageal puncture to facilitate future voice rehabilitation. No lymph node metastasis was found on pathological examination of the dissected cervical nodes.

The patient, a 72-year-old male, underwent surgery for an extensive carcinoma of the hypopharynx, which had invaded the adjacent esophageal inlet. A combined pharyngolaryngectomy and esophagectomy were conducted, followed by reconstruction using a gastric pull-up technique. The tumor was noted to extend into the cervical esophagus, and metastatic spread was seen in three out of 15 lymph nodes. Postoperative care included nutritional support and preparations for adjuvant chemoradiotherapy.

A 54-year-old female presented with a carcinoma of the soft palate. The mass measured 2.5 cm and was confined to the mucosal surface without deep infiltration. An excision of the soft palate tumor was performed with clear margins. A selective neck dissection was completed and showed no evidence of nodal metastasis. The patient recovered well and was referred for speech therapy to manage palatal function post-surgery.

The patient, a 60-year-old male, underwent surgical resection for an oropharyngeal carcinoma, primarily located at the base of the tongue. The tumor was moderately differentiated and measured 4 cm, with invasion into the lingual tonsil. A transoral robotic surgery (TORS) approach was used for precision and minimal invasiveness. A modified radical neck dissection was also performed, revealing metastatic involvement in one out of ten lymph nodes. The postoperative course included assessment for further chemoradiation.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, underwent a partial laryngectomy for a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the right vocal cord. The tumor was well-circumscribed and localized without invasion into the surrounding laryngeal structures. Intraoperative frozen section analysis confirmed clear surgical margins. No lymphovascular invasion was noted. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was extubated in the operating room. Post-operative care included voice therapy and nutritional support.

A 58-year-old female presented with a mass in the left tonsillar region. A transoral robotic surgery (TORS) was performed to excise the oropharyngeal tumor, which was confirmed preoperatively via biopsy to be a poorly differentiated squamous cell carcinoma. The tumor extended into the base of the tongue. Additionally, a selective neck dissection was carried out, revealing metastatic involvement of level II lymph nodes. Post-surgical recovery was uneventful, and the patient was referred for adjuvant radiotherapy.

The patient is a 70-year-old male with a history of tobacco use presenting with a lesion on the floor of the mouth. An excisional biopsy revealed moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma, prompting a wide local excision and ipsilateral neck dissection. The surgical specimen showed clear margins, and two out of twenty lymph nodes were positive for metastasis. The patient was advised to follow up for adjuvant chemotherapy.

A 65-year-old female with a progressive dysphagia and hoarseness was found to have a mass in the hypopharynx on endoscopic examination. A total laryngopharyngectomy with bilateral neck dissection was performed. The pathology report confirmed a T3 hypopharyngeal squamous cell carcinoma with bilateral cervical lymph node involvement. The patient had an uneventful postoperative course and was referred for comprehensive rehabilitation and nutritional support.

The patient, a 55-year-old male, underwent a mandibulectomy and free flap reconstruction for an infiltrative squamous cell carcinoma of the lower gingiva. The tumor extended into the adjacent mandible, necessitating marginal resection. Surgical margins were clear, and there was no evidence of regional lymph node metastasis. The patient was closely monitored in the intensive care unit post-operatively and discharged with a plan for outpatient rehabilitation.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, underwent a partial laryngectomy for a T2 squamous cell carcinoma located in the glottic region of the larynx. The tumor measured 2.5 cm and was confined to the true vocal cords without extension to the supraglottic or subglottic areas. Preservation of the voice box was achieved, with clear resection margins confirmed intraoperatively. No enlarged cervical lymph nodes were noted during the neck dissection, and the procedure was completed without complications.

A 55-year-old female presented with a 3 cm ulcerative lesion on the lateral border of the tongue, diagnosed as squamous cell carcinoma. A partial glossectomy was performed, with the resection extending to the base of the tongue. The lesion was found to invade the adjacent floor of the mouth. A selective neck dissection was also conducted, revealing metastatic involvement of two ipsilateral cervical lymph nodes. The surgical site was reconstructed using a radial forearm free flap.

The patient, a 68-year-old male, underwent a comprehensive procedure for an extensive T4 carcinoma of the oropharynx, primarily involving the left tonsil and extending into the soft palate. The surgical intervention included a left radical tonsillectomy and resection of the involved soft palate tissue. Intraoperative frozen sections confirmed tumor-free margins. Additionally, a bilateral neck dissection was performed, revealing metastatic spread to the contralateral cervical lymph nodes. The patient required a temporary tracheostomy post-procedure for airway management.

A 45-year-old male with a diagnosis of verrucous carcinoma of the buccal mucosa underwent a wide local excision of the lesion. The tumor was 4 cm in diameter and involved the right buccal mucosa extending to the retromolar trigone. The surgery was performed with clear margins, and no lymph node involvement was noted on preoperative imaging. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient retained full function of the oral cavity.

The patient, a 71-year-old female, underwent a total pharyngectomy with reconstruction for a T3 hypopharyngeal carcinoma. The tumor involved the posterior pharyngeal wall and extended to the pyriform sinus. A total laryngectomy was also necessary due to the tumor's proximity to the laryngeal inlet. A gastric pull-up procedure was used for reconstruction. A bilateral neck dissection was conducted, with metastatic involvement detected in the right cervical lymph nodes. The surgery was completed successfully, though the patient required a permanent stoma.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, presented with a primary squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. A total laryngectomy was performed, followed by a partial pharyngectomy due to extension of the tumor into the pharynx. Intraoperative findings confirmed the involvement of the anterior commissure and right vocal cord, necessitating resection. The surgical margins were clear upon frozen section analysis. Bilateral neck dissections were also performed, revealing metastatic involvement of the right level II and III lymph nodes. Post-operative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was referred for adjuvant radiotherapy.

A 55-year-old female with a T2N1M0 carcinoma of the left tonsil underwent a transoral robotic-assisted resection of the oropharyngeal tumor. The lesion measured 3.5 cm and was confined to the tonsillar region, with no invasion to surrounding structures. A selective neck dissection was performed, revealing a single metastatic node in level II. The surgical margins were negative for tumor, and the patient tolerated the procedure well. The patient was scheduled for follow-up imaging and potential adjuvant therapy.

The patient, a 68-year-old male, was diagnosed with a carcinoma of the floor of the mouth. A composite resection was performed, including partial mandibulectomy and resection of the floor of the mouth with reconstruction using a radial forearm free flap. The tumor had involved the lingual nerve, which was sacrificed during the procedure. Bilateral neck dissections were carried out, and no lymphatic spread was observed. The post-operative course was complicated by a minor wound infection, which resolved with antibiotics.

A 47-year-old male presented with a large ulcerated mass in the hypopharynx, confirmed to be a poorly differentiated carcinoma. The patient underwent a total pharyngolaryngectomy with bilateral neck dissections. The tumor had invaded the prevertebral fascia but not breached it. Histopathology indicated involvement of bilateral level IV nodes. Reconstruction was performed using a jejunal free flap. The patient was started on enteral nutrition post-operatively and is planned for adjuvant chemoradiotherapy.

The patient, a 59-year-old female, presented with a moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma of the buccal mucosa. A wide local excision of the primary tumor was performed, along with a flap reconstruction using a buccal fat pad flap. No neck dissection was required as preoperative imaging showed no evidence of nodal involvement. The surgical margins were clear, and the patient was discharged on post-operative day three with instructions for a soft diet.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, presented with a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the glottic larynx. A partial laryngectomy was performed to excise the tumor. Intraoperative findings indicated that the tumor was confined to the left vocal fold, with no extension to the anterior commissure or subglottic space. Margins were clear upon frozen section analysis. The surgical field was closed with absorbable sutures, and the patient was extubated without complications.

A 55-year-old female with a history of smoking presented with a mass in the right tonsil. Imaging and biopsy confirmed a T3 squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx. The patient underwent a transoral robotic surgery (TORS) for tumor resection, followed by a selective neck dissection of levels II-IV on the right side due to suspected lymph node involvement. Pathology confirmed metastatic involvement in one lymph node, with extranodal extension. The surgical margins were negative.

A 68-year-old male with a long-standing history of alcohol use presented with a large ulcerated lesion on the floor of the mouth. Biopsy confirmed a T4a squamous cell carcinoma. A composite resection was performed, including partial mandibulectomy and excision of the tumor. A radial forearm free flap was used for reconstruction. No gross lymphadenopathy was noted intraoperatively. Post-operative recovery was uneventful.

The patient, a 47-year-old female, was diagnosed with a T1 carcinoma of the left base of tongue. The patient underwent a minimally invasive transoral laser microsurgery to excise the lesion. The margins were negative for carcinoma. No neck dissection was performed, as there was no clinical or radiological evidence of nodal metastasis. The patient was discharged on the second postoperative day.

A 70-year-old male presented with hoarseness and difficulty swallowing. Laryngoscopy revealed a T3 tumor involving the hypopharynx, extending into the esophageal inlet. The patient underwent a total laryngopharyngectomy with bilateral neck dissection. Multiple lymph nodes were found to be metastatic on the right side, without extranodal extension. A jejunostomy tube was placed for feeding.

The patient underwent a partial glossectomy for a T2 squamous cell carcinoma located on the left lateral border of the tongue. The tumor measured approximately 2.5 cm and extended into the muscular layer, necessitating careful excision to ensure clear margins. Intraoperatively, frozen sections were utilized to confirm adequate clearance of malignant tissue. The submandibular lymph nodes were also dissected, given the tumor's proximity to the regional lymphatic drainage. No macroscopic residual disease was observed post-resection, and the patient was extubated without complications.

The patient presented with a bulky mass in the right tonsil, diagnosed as a T3 carcinoma of the oropharynx. A transoral robotic-assisted resection of the tumor was performed, followed by a comprehensive neck dissection. The tumor invaded the surrounding soft tissues but was successfully excised with negative margins. Multiple lymph nodes were involved, and a selective neck dissection of levels I-III was carried out. Postoperative recovery was uneventful, and the patient was referred for adjuvant radiotherapy.

The patient, a 62-year-old male, underwent a total laryngectomy for an advanced T4a laryngeal carcinoma involving the left vocal cord. The tumor had invaded the thyroid cartilage and extended to the extralaryngeal tissues. A tracheostomy was established intraoperatively for airway management. The surgical margins were clear, and a right-sided neck dissection was performed, revealing metastatic spread to several lymph nodes. The patient tolerated the procedure well and was transferred to the recovery unit in stable condition.

A 47-year-old female underwent a composite resection for a T1 squamous cell carcinoma of the floor of the mouth, measuring 1 cm in greatest dimension. The lesion was confined to the mucosa with no evidence of deeper invasion. Margins were confirmed to be clear via frozen section analysis. Given the early stage of the disease, no neck dissection was deemed necessary. Postoperative recovery was swift, and the patient was discharged the following day with instructions for follow-up surveillance.

The patient was admitted for surgical management of a T2 carcinoma of the hypopharynx. A partial pharyngectomy was performed, with reconstruction using a radial forearm free flap to restore pharyngeal continuity. The tumor was located in the pyriform sinus and was resected with adequate margins. A bilateral neck dissection was carried out, revealing no lymph node involvement. The patient was closely monitored postoperatively and demonstrated satisfactory healing of the graft site.

The patient underwent a total laryngectomy with bilateral neck dissection for a T3N2b squamous cell carcinoma of the larynx. The tumor involved the true vocal cords and extended into the thyroid cartilage. Intraoperative frozen sections confirmed negative margins. Bilateral neck dissection revealed metastatic involvement of level II and III lymph nodes on the left side. The right neck was clear of metastatic disease. Reconstruction was achieved with a pectoralis major flap due to the extensive resection.

A partial glossectomy was performed for a T2 squamous cell carcinoma of the left lateral border of the tongue. The tumor extended to the floor of the mouth but did not cross the midline. Margins were obtained and found to be negative on frozen section. The patient also underwent selective neck dissection of levels I-III on the left side, which revealed no lymph node metastasis.

The patient presented with a T4a squamous cell carcinoma of the maxillary sinus with extension into the orbit and skin. A maxillectomy with orbital exenteration and resection of the involved skin was performed. The resection margins were negative, and reconstruction was achieved with an anterolateral thigh free flap. Regional nodes were not dissected during this procedure as imaging showed no evidence of metastasis.

An extensive resection was performed for a T3 squamous cell carcinoma of the hypopharynx involving the piriform sinus. The procedure included a pharyngolaryngectomy with bilateral neck dissection. Metastatic disease was noted in the right level II and III nodes. Reconstruction of the pharyngeal defect was achieved with a radial forearm free flap.

The patient underwent a wide local excision of a T1 squamous cell carcinoma of the buccal mucosa. The tumor was superficial, involving only the mucosa with clear margins obtained on frozen section. No neck dissection was performed as imaging and clinical examination showed no regional lymph node involvement.
